text:
An octopus escaped from the National Aquarium in New Zealand. It got out of its tank, slid down a 50-metre drainpipe, and reached the sea.

The octopus was named Inky. He is a common New Zealand octopus. Workers think the tank lid was left a little open by mistake. In the middle of the night, when no one was there, Inky climbed out of the tank. He went down the side and moved across the aquarium floor.

Rob Yarrell, a manager at the aquarium in Napier, said octopuses are good at escaping. He said Inky was not unhappy or lonely, because octopuses like to be alone. He said Inky was very curious and wanted to see what was outside.

One idea is that Inky slid three or four metres across the floor and went into a drainpipe that led to the sea. The pipe was 50 metres long and opened into Hawke’s Bay, on the east coast of New Zealand’s North Island.

Another idea is that Inky squeezed into an open pipe at the top of his tank. This pipe went under the floor and into the drain. Yarrell said he was surprised when they came in the next morning and the tank was empty. He said the staff felt sad, but Inky often surprised them. Yarrell has not started a search for Inky.

Reiss Jenkinson, an exhibits keeper, said he was sure no one took Inky. He said he knows how octopuses act. He said he has seen octopuses on boats slip through bilge pumps. He also said the security is too strong for someone to take Inky.

Octopuses have no bones, so they can fit into very small spaces. They have been filmed squeezing through gaps the size of coins. Octopuses are also very smart and can use tools. In Wellington, at the Island Bay Marine Education Centre, one octopus used to visit another tank at night to steal crabs, then go back to its own tank. Another octopus there, named Ozymandias, was thought to have opened a jar very fast before it was released into the ocean.

A local fisherman brought Inky to the aquarium years ago. The fisherman found Inky caught in a crayfish pot. Yarrell said Inky looked rough and had scars and shortened limbs. He said Inky had been living on a reef and fighting with fish, so he was not in good shape. Yarrell said Inky was about the size of a rugby ball and was unusually intelligent. He was friendly, curious, and popular with visitors. The aquarium has another octopus named Blotchy, but Blotchy is smaller, and Inky had a bigger personality.

The aquarium does not plan to add more security because Inky’s escape was a one-off. But the staff now pay more attention to what octopuses can do. The aquarium is not looking for a new Inky, but it might take another octopus if a fisherman brought one in. Yarrell said there is always a chance Inky could come back.

text:
Flappy Bird
Be careful what you wish for, especially when you make something new. Dong Nguyen made the mobile game Flappy Bird. He later removed it from app stores. He said the game’s success had ruined his simple life. It had been downloaded more than 50 million times. It was making him about £30,000 a day from ads. He wrote on Twitter, “I cannot take this anymore.”

Regretting a game is not the same as regretting a rifle. But Nguyen is one of many inventors who wish they had not made something that caused problems.

The labradoodle
The labradoodle is cute. But the problem is how crossbreed dogs have been bred and sold since Wally Conron created the labradoodle in the 1980s. He told the Associated Press, “I’ve done a lot of damage. I’ve created a lot of problems. There are a lot of unhealthy and abandoned dogs out there.” Conron made the labradoodle while working for the Royal Guide Dog Association of Australia. He wanted a dog for a blind woman. Her husband was allergic to dog hair. Conron did not expect the labradoodle, and other poodle-cross dogs, to become so popular. Many of these dogs have health problems.

The AK-47
Six months before he died in December 2013, Mikhail Kalashnikov wrote to the head of the Russian Orthodox Church. Kalashnikov designed the AK-47 assault rifle. He wrote that his suffering was very strong. He asked if he was responsible for deaths caused by his rifle, even if the people killed were enemies.

Electronic tagging
Electronic tags were first planned in the 1960s. They were meant to track former prisoners at school and work. They were also meant to reward good behaviour. The inventors were Bob Gable and his brother Kirkland. Later, they were shocked that tags became a way to control and punish people. In 2010, Kirkland Gable told the Guardian, “It’s not pleasant, but I’m not in control of the universe. I have to realize there are some things out of my control.”

Pepper spray
In 2011, police used pepper spray on peaceful protesters at a University of California campus. After this, one of the scientists who helped develop pepper spray in the 1980s spoke against how it was used. Kamran Loghman told The New York Times, “I have never seen such an inappropriate and improper use of chemical agents.”

The office cubicle
In the late 1960s, a new kind of office was created. It was meant to give workers privacy and help them work better by giving them more space. Instead, companies used cubicles to squeeze workers into smaller spaces. Cubicles became a sign of all workers being the same and work feeling empty. In 1997, the inventor Bob Propst said, “the cubiclizing of people in modern corporations is monolithic insanity.”

text:
JMW Turner, one of Britain’s greatest painters, will be on the new £20 note after a nationwide vote.

This is the first time an artist has been on a British banknote. The governor of the Bank of England, Mark Carney, asked people to name a dead cultural figure who should get this honour.

Turner is famous for dramatic paintings of the sea. He beat 590 other people who were suggested, including painters, sculptors, fashion designers, photographers, film-makers, and actors. About 30,000 members of the public sent in names.

The long list included Alfred Hitchcock, Alexander McQueen, Derek Jarman, Laura Ashley, William Morris, and Vanessa Bell. A group of artists, critics, and historians cut the list down to five.

The final five were Barbara Hepworth, Charlie Chaplin, Josiah Wedgwood, William Hogarth, and Turner. They were chosen because they made a clear contribution to visual arts and British society, and because their influence has lasted.

The new banknote was announced at the Turner Contemporary gallery in Margate. The gallery is on the place where Mrs Booth’s lodging house used to be, where Turner always stayed when he visited. Carney and the artist Tracey Emin, who grew up in Margate, made the announcement together.

Carney said it was important to have a proper process that involved the public. He said banknotes are not only practical, and that if done well, they can be “a piece of art in everyone’s pocket”. He also said, “Money is memory for a country and its people,” and that banknotes celebrate the UK’s heritage and culture.

Emin said that having Turner on the £20 note shows that British people value creativity and the arts.

The note will show Turner’s self-portrait from 1799, which is in Tate Britain. It will also show one of his most famous paintings, The Fighting Temeraire. This painting is about a ship that played an important part in Nelson’s victory at the Battle of Trafalgar in 1805.

The note will also have a quote from Turner, “light is therefore colour,” and his signature. The signature is taken from his will, where he left many of his works to the nation.

The new £20 note will replace the one with Adam Smith. It will start being used by 2020. This is the first time the public has been able to help choose whose face is on a British banknote.

Carney changed the process in 2013. This happened after a well-known campaign to keep women, apart from the Queen, on banknotes, after the Bank decided to stop using the Elizabeth Fry £5 note.

Historical people first appeared on banknotes in 1970. Turner will join Winston Churchill and Jane Austen on new polymer notes, made from a plastic-type material. Churchill is on the £5 note and Austen is on the £10 note. Austen, the Pride and Prejudice author, is only the third woman to be on British money, after Florence Nightingale and Elizabeth Fry.

Turner was born in 1775 in London. His father was a barber. Turner started at the Royal Academy Schools when he was 14. In 1786 he was sent to Margate, and he began to love painting and drawing the north-east Kent coast. He went back there many times and painted some of his most dramatic oil paintings and watercolours. He called its skies “the loveliest in all Europe”.

Turner made a lot of art in his life: more than 550 oil paintings and 2,000 watercolours. A film about his life was made in 2014 by Mike Leigh, with Timothy Spall playing Turner.

Victoria Pomery, the director of Turner Contemporary, said the choice to honour Turner is extraordinary. She said the public vote shows Turner is the nation’s favourite artist, and it is an official sign of the great historical value of visual arts in the UK. She also said it reminds the UK to value creative people in the future and to invest to support the next great artists and innovators.

text:
The rule says the 8-hour work day is finished. The most important work is done, and the rest can wait until morning. Many workers would think it is time to go home.

But for millions of Japanese workers, leaving on time and being home for dinner can lead to people saying they are not loyal.

For many years, companies in Japan have expected workers to do as much work as possible. Now the government is challenging this overwork culture. It is thinking about a new law that would require workers to take at least five days of paid holiday each year.

Japanese workers are supposed to get an average of 18.5 paid holiday days a year. That is only two days less than the world average. They must get at least 10 days, and there are also 15 one-day national holidays. But in real life, few workers take all their days. The labour ministry says they usually use only nine days out of the 18.5 days. Many British workers see a two-week summer holiday as a basic right. In Japan, some workers think even a four-night trip to Hawaii is too self-indulgent.

This plan will be debated in the current parliament session. It comes after companies started telling workers to nap at work to improve performance.

By the end of the decade, the government hopes the law, if it passes, will push Japanese workers to act more like British workers, who use about 20 paid leave days a year, and French workers, who use about 25.

Japan’s strict work culture may have helped it become a strong economy. People around the world respect Japanese workers for their commitment to their companies. But this often leaves little time for anything else.

Japan has a low birth rate, and experts say the population may fall quickly. Part of the blame is that couples do not have enough time to start families. More workers are getting sick from stress. Some die from karoshi, which means death from overwork.

Studies suggest that longer hours at work do not always make people more productive. Still, many workers keep doing very long hours, like in the 1980s.

About 22% of Japanese workers work more than 49 hours a week. In the US it is 16%, and in France and Germany it is 11%, based on Japanese government data. South Korea is worse, with 35%.

Erika Sekiguchi works 14 hours a day and gives up many paid holidays, and she is not even an extreme example. She is 36 and works at a trading company. In 2014, she used eight of her 20 paid vacation days, and six of those days were sick leave. She said, “Nobody else uses their vacation days.”

She has the same problem as many workers in Japan. If they take time off to rest, they may be criticized. If they do not, they cannot recharge.

Yuu Wakebe is a health ministry official who works on policy about working hours. He admits he does 100 hours of overtime each month. He says there is strong pressure to match co-workers hour for hour. He said, “It is a worker’s right to take paid vacations. But working in Japan involves quite a lot of volunteer spirit.”

People say fear of being left out at work is causing more stress-related illness, early death, and suicide. Official data says about 200 people die each year from heart attacks, strokes, and other karoshi events caused by very hard work schedules.

The prime minister, Shinzo Abe, is not known for taking long vacations. But even he has spoken against unreasonable demands that companies put on workers as they try to survive in a more complex global market. Abe recently said Japan’s work culture “falsely beatifies long hours”.

text:
John Lewis made a Christmas advert in 2015 about a lonely old man who lives in a crater on the moon. The store also wanted the advert to help a charity.

Many people get excited about the John Lewis Christmas advert. For some people, it feels like the start of Christmas shopping. John Lewis said it would try to raise hundreds of thousands of pounds for Age UK. It also asked staff and customers to join their local Age UK branch to help older people who might be alone during the holiday.

John Lewis spent £7m on the campaign. It included the TV advert, a smartphone game, and items to buy, like glow-in-the-dark pyjamas. In 11 stores, there were areas decorated to look like the moon.

In the past two years, the adverts had cute animals: a bear and hare, and then a penguin. This time, the story is about a young girl named Lily. She sees an old man living in a small shack on the moon through her telescope. Lily tries to contact him by sending a letter and by shooting a note with a bow and arrow. Then she sends him a present: a telescope tied to balloons. At last, they can make contact.

The advert’s line is: “Show someone they’re loved this Christmas”. This is similar to Age UK’s message: “No one should have no one at Christmas”. Profits from three products, a mug, a gift tag, and a card, will go to the charity.

Rachel Swift, head of marketing at John Lewis, said the charity connects with people at this time of year. She said the advert can make people think about someone on their street who might not see anybody.

The campaign uses the Oasis song Half the World Away, sung in a new way by Norwegian artist Aurora.

The advert cost £1m to make. The moon scenes were filmed at Warner Bros Studios, where the Harry Potter films were made. The set was built by someone from the team behind the Star Wars film The Force Awakens.

John Lewis also shared teasers on TV and social media using the hashtag #OnTheMoon. There was a full moon on Christmas Day 2015, and Swift said this was just a coincidence.

In 2014, John Lewis also spent £7m on a campaign. It had a realistic animated penguin and a young boy, with the song Real Love by John Lennon, sung by Tom Odell. By the first week of January, it had 22m views on YouTube. That was more than Sainsbury’s advert from 2014, which had 16.6m views and showed First World War soldiers sharing a bar of chocolate.

Swift said John Lewis has used the same plan for the last five years. She said it is about giving thoughtful gifts and doing extra for someone you love at Christmas. She said they do not start by thinking the advert will be huge. They try to make something right for the brand and something customers will love.

Sarah Vizard, news editor at Marketing Week, said John Lewis seemed quieter in stores this time, even though more companies are competing. She said many brands tried to copy John Lewis by making emotional and creative adverts, but then went back to showing what you can buy in the store. She said John Lewis still does the emotional story best, and she thought people would find the advert really cute.

Other Christmas adverts also tried to get attention. Waitrose, which is connected to John Lewis, put its advert online before it appeared on TV during The X Factor. It used the same agency that has made John Lewis Christmas adverts for years.

Burberry released its second Christmas film, with Romeo Beckham and Elton John. Currys used Jeff Goldblum for its first special Christmas advert. Asda, Lidl, and Morrisons also started their campaigns at the beginning of November.

Marc Bolland, chief executive of Marks & Spencer, said that in the first week of September, the most searched word on its website was Christmas.

Boots also started its Christmas campaign in early November. It went back to a more traditional style after its praised 2014 advert about a family doing a lot to be together at Christmas.

text:
The last time Kate Bush performed on stage, the mobile phone was still being tested. Now, 35 years later, she is performing again in a very different world.

At many concerts today, people hold up phones and tablets. But Kate Bush does not want fans to watch her show through a screen.

Before her concerts at the Hammersmith Apollo in London, Bush wrote a message to fans. She asked them to put away their phones at her gigs. She said she chose a small, close theatre, not a big stadium. She asked people not to take photos or film during the show. She said she wants to connect with the audience, not with iPhones, iPads, or cameras. She knows it is a big request, but she thinks it will help everyone share the experience together.

Bush also likes theatre and fancy costumes. She may also want to stop unclear videos going on YouTube, so the show stays a surprise for the thousands of fans who bought tickets for the 22 dates.

Other artists have also complained about phones at concerts. Roger Daltrey from The Who said it is “weird” when people watch the screen instead of the artist on stage. He said he feels sorry for them, because they are not fully in the moment.

In 2013, Beyoncé told a fan to stop filming. She said he could not sing because he was too busy recording. She told him to put the camera down and enjoy the moment.

This debate is not only about music. Sports fans also talk about it. Dutch fans at PSV Eindhoven protested against wi-fi in their stadium. They held banners that said “No wi-fi. Support the team,” “You can sit at home,” and “Stand united”. Manchester United also told fans to leave “large electronic devices” at home, and they banned filming on tablets this season.

Jarvis Cocker said phones in the audience made him “insane at concerts”. He said it seems stupid to look at a small screen when something is happening right in front of you.

Johnny Marr said in 2013 that people miss the full feeling of live music when they only try to record it. He said watching through your phone is a wasted chance. He told people to put their phone down and enjoy the gig. He said gigs are also about the people around you, and watching on a little screen is a waste of time.

The Yeah Yeah Yeahs put up a sign at one venue. It asked fans not to watch the show through a screen. It told them to put devices away as a courtesy to the person behind them and to the band.

Phones have also caused problems in classical music. In June 2013, the pianist Krystian Zimerman walked off stage because a fan was filming on a smartphone. He came back and said: “The destruction of music because of YouTube is enormous.”

But Sam Watt of Vyclone, an app that encourages filming at concerts, said these artists are fighting a losing battle. He said filming is now part of concerts, and it can improve the experience. In the app, people upload their videos, and the app mixes them with other people’s videos to make one crowd video. He said this can create great content.

Watt also said filming adds to the experience, not takes away from it. He said he could have an interesting discussion with Kate Bush and might change her mind. He said people want memories, and artists should accept that fans will film.

text:
Cities do not usually pick up and move. But Kiruna, a town in northern Sweden in the Arctic, must move. If it does not, the ground could sink and the town could fall into the earth.

Krister Lindstedt from White, an architecture firm in Stockholm, says the town has a hard choice. A big iron-ore mine is eating away the ground under the streets. If the mine stops, many people will lose jobs. If the town does not move, it could be destroyed.

Kiruna was started in 1900 by the state-owned mining company Luossavaara-Kiirunavaara (LK). The town became rich because of the iron ore under it. Deputy Mayor Niklas Siren says the town exists because of the mine.

Kiruna is 145 km inside the Arctic Circle. Winters are very harsh. There is no sunlight in winter, and average temperatures are below -15C. But people stayed because the magnetite ore is valuable. The mine is the world’s largest underground iron-ore mine. It produces 90% of Europe’s iron, enough to build more than six Eiffel Towers a day. Demand is still growing.

In 2004, the mining company told the town that it was in danger. As the mine digs toward the city at a 60-degree angle, the ground will sink. Buildings could crack and collapse. Ten years later, cracks are starting to appear in the ground and are getting closer to town.

Viktoria Walldin, a social anthropologist working with the architects, says people have been waiting and worrying for ten years. Many have not made big choices, like buying a house, redecorating, having a child, or opening a business.

After years of delay, the city showed its main plan. Lindstedt says the town will move like a “walking millipede.” Streets and squares will slowly move east along a new main street. The town should be out of danger by 2033.

A new town square is already being built 3 km to the east. A new town hall is planned by Danish architect Henning Larsen. Twenty important buildings will be taken apart and put back together in the new place, piece by piece. The rust-red wooden church, built in 1912 and shaped like Sami teepees, will go into a new park. The cast-iron bell tower will stand again near the town hall. But not everything can be saved.

Walldin says small places also matter to people. She spoke to an old woman who walks past a bench where she had her first kiss. Places like that, or the hospital where your first child was born, help people feel who they are. Many of these places will disappear.

The project is called “the most democratic move in history.” The mining company will pay about £320m to build new facilities, like a high school, fire station, community centre, library, and swimming hall. But many people mainly worry about where they will live and how homes will be given out.

Lindstedt says these details are not decided yet. He says people are used to low rents and high incomes, but this may change. LK has agreed to pay residents the value of their homes plus 25%. Many locals say this is not enough to buy a new house at market prices.

To help decide home values, the architects studied housing rents in nearby cities for years. They also marked Kiruna homes by what they have, like size, gardens, and closeness to bus stops and the city centre. They also suggested a “Kiruna Portal,” a big place to save materials from houses that will be torn down, so they might be reused in the new town. But Sweden does not have a strong tradition of people building their own homes, so it may not happen.

The new town plan looks very different from the old Kiruna. The current town has many winding streets and separate houses with gardens. The new plan has more people living close together, in multi-storey apartment buildings around shared courtyards. The streets are straighter, and cold winds may blow strongly along them.

The architects say this is a chance for Kiruna to start again as a sustainable town. They hope it will attract young people, with new cultural places and ideas like a cable car over the main street. But many current residents may not be able to afford this new vision.

text:
Lego made much more profit in the first half of 2014. This was helped by The Lego Movie, which was very popular in the US and UK.

The Danish toy company sold more toys in Europe, the Americas, and Asia. Many children bought Lego sets linked to the movie. The movie came out in February. By the first weekend in April, it had made more than $250m in the US and £31m in the UK.

The movie cost about $60m to make. People said it was almost perfect marketing, because it was fun to watch and also made people want to buy Lego products.

Lego’s finance director, John Goodwin, said the movie toys helped results in the first half of 2014. He said they would see what happens after the movie is released on DVD in the second half of 2014.

Lego’s operating profit for the first six months rose by 12% to $630m. Sales rose by 11% and were more than three times higher than six years earlier.

Lego’s chief executive, Jørgen Vig Knudstorp, said the result was very good, even though the economy was tough. He said it came from Lego’s ability to design, launch, and deliver products that children around the world really want.

Lego is based in Billund. It began making plastic bricks in 1949. By the 1970s, it was a common children’s toy around the world. But the company had big problems and was close to failing in 2003. Knudstorp became chief executive, ending 70 years of family control. He removed hundreds of extra products so the company could focus again on its famous bricks.

In April, the company opened its first factory in China and opened an office in Shanghai to lead growth in the world’s second-biggest economy.

text:
Loneliness is now a big issue. The Office for National Statistics says Britain is the loneliest country in Europe. People in Britain are less likely to have strong friendships or know their neighbours than people in other EU countries. Many also say they have no one to rely on in a crisis. Professor John Cacioppo at the University of Chicago found that loneliness is very bad for older people’s health. He said it is twice as bad as obesity, and it can cause death almost as much as poverty.

But these studies can miss a big problem: loneliness in young adults. In 2010, the Mental Health Foundation found that loneliness worried young people more than older people. In the survey, people aged 18 to 34 were more likely than people over 55 to feel lonely often, worry about being alone, and feel depressed because of loneliness.

“Loneliness is a known problem among older people – there are day centres and charities to help them,” says Sam Challis from the mental health charity Mind. “But when young people reach 21, they’re too old for youth services.” This is a problem because loneliness is closely linked to mental health. It is linked to more stress, depression, paranoia, anxiety, addiction, cognitive decline, and it is a known factor in suicide. In a new essay, Paul Farmer from Mind and Jenny Edwards from the Mental Health Foundation say loneliness can both cause mental health problems and be caused by them.

So what can young people do about loneliness? Dr Grant Blank at the Oxford Internet Institute says social media and the internet can help, but they can also cause problems. They help when they let us talk to loved ones who are far away. They are not helpful when they replace meeting people face to face. Challis says people often show an “ideal” version of themselves online, and we expect our lives to look like what we see in the media. When we compare our life to friends’ perfect-looking lives, we may pull away from other people.

Meditation and apps like Headspace are popular and often suggested for mental health problems. But they may not help loneliness, because they encourage us to sit alone with our thoughts. Challis says it is better to look at the real reasons you feel lonely first, and ask what is stopping you from going out and seeing people.

A 2013 University of Michigan study about social media found that Facebook can reduce life satisfaction. But using technology to help you meet new people can help. Also, if you cannot go outside, the internet can bring comfort. Mumsnet was “an absolute godsend” for Maddy Matthews, 19, a student with a two-month-old daughter. After the birth, she rarely sees her university friends, and her partner works most evenings. She says that in the first days she was up late feeding the baby and worried she was doing something wrong. Posting on Mumsnet helped her feel less alone.

Helplines can also reduce loneliness, at least for a short time. One in four men who call the support charity Samaritans mention loneliness or isolation. Get Connected is a free confidential helpline for young people, where they can get help with emotional and mental health issues linked to loneliness. Websites like Mind’s also offer support services to remind you that you are not alone.

At work, it can help to tell your employer how you feel. John Binns, who advises businesses on mental health and well-being, went to hospital for stress-related depression in 2007 and took two months off work. He felt there was no one to talk to, and he was not close enough to his colleagues for them to notice changes in his behaviour. Being more open with his employer and colleagues made it easier to return to work. He says colleagues are often more supportive than people expect. His colleagues reached out, asked him to lunch, and told him that things had not changed too much since he had been away.

Small talk in the office may seem like a waste of time, but it can protect us from some of the emotional and mental effects of work stress. Rick Hughes from the British Association for Counselling and Psychotherapy (BACP) says that if you connect with your team, you might feel stressed but not isolated.

Nicky Forsythe, a psychotherapist and the founder of Talk for Health, says the social networks we have may seem unimportant, but they are key to our well-being. She says the most important thing is to have a regular time and place to think about your life, and to have a kind, understanding listener.

Counselling can help people learn skills like empathy. The BACP website lets you search for counsellors near you. Hughes says that sharing a problem can help, and sometimes you need to talk to someone who is separate from your friends and family. Most universities offer counselling to students, and many also run group sessions that focus on loneliness.

If recent research is right, loneliness is killing older people. Because the population is getting older, we should try to reduce loneliness before it is too late. Ruth Sutherland, the chief executive of the relationship counselling service Relate, says getting older does not have to mean getting lonelier. But she says this depends a lot on building good, strong relationships earlier in life.

text:
US researchers say a nasal spray with oxytocin, sometimes called the “love hormone,” might help children with autism deal with social situations better. Brain scans showed that one dose changed how the brain reacted to facial expressions. This change could make social time feel more natural and rewarding.

The researchers said that a course of oxytocin might make behavior therapies work better. These therapies already help people with autism practice social skills. Kevin Pelphrey, director of the Child Neuroscience Lab at Yale University, said that over time children might respond more appropriately in social situations, want to interact more, make more eye contact, and talk more.

Autism is a developmental disorder seen in more than 1 in 100 people. It affects people in different ways, but often includes trouble with social interaction and communication. There is no established treatment for the social problems caused by autism.

Yale researchers have studied oxytocin because it is important for bonding and trust. Results from other studies have been mixed. One recent study found no clear benefit when youths took oxytocin for several days. Pelphrey said oxytocin might help the brain learn from social experiences, and might work best together with therapies that encourage social engagement. He said it could act like a “social enhancer” when combined with behavioral treatment.

In this study, scientists used functional MRI to scan the brains of 17 youths ages 8 to 16 with autism. The youths looked at pictures of cars or at the eyes of people showing different emotions. The scans were done 45 minutes after the youths inhaled either a placebo or oxytocin through a nasal spray.

The scans showed that parts of the brain linked to reward acted more normally after oxytocin. These areas were more active when the youths looked at faces and less active when they looked at cars. The study was published in the journal Proceedings of the National Academy of Sciences in the United States.

Pelphrey said that if other studies get the same result, oxytocin might help with a key problem in autism: social motivation. But he warned it is too early to use oxytocin as a treatment. He also warned people not to buy oxytocin online or try it at home, because they may not know what they are buying. He said more follow-up research is needed.

Simon Baron-Cohen, director of the Autism Research Centre at Cambridge University, said the study is important because it shows changes in brain activity in areas involved in social thinking after oxytocin is given. But he also said doctors should be careful until we know much more about the benefits and risks, in much larger studies.

A surprising result was that the oxytocin spray did not improve how the youths did on the social task. It is also not clear if oxytocin only helps people with autism or if it has unwanted side effects. The effects last only about 45 minutes, so it may be hard to use as a treatment.

Uta Frith at University College London said the study suggests oxytocin may make faces more interesting, because reward-related brain areas became more active. But she said this change was only seen in brain activity, not in behavior. Showing a change in behavior will be very important if the nasal spray is to be useful.

text:
James Bond is the hero of one of the longest and most successful movie series in the world. There are 23 Bond movies, and they have made more than $6bn worldwide. The Bond movies are still very popular.

The movie Skyfall did extremely well. When it came out in 2012, it became the biggest film ever at the UK box office. It earned $1.1bn worldwide and is now ninth on the list of the biggest-earning films of all time.

Because of Skyfall, many people were excited to hear news about the 24th Bond film. Its title is Spectre. People ask: can Spectre be as successful as Skyfall? Will Skyfall stay the best, or can Spectre do even better?

Charles Gant, film editor for Heat magazine, says things look good. He says Skyfall was a smart movie because it felt modern and also old-fashioned. It pleased newer fans from the Daniel Craig era and also older fans who missed the classic Bond style. Gant thinks the title Spectre shows they want to keep older fans and also keep younger fans.

Eon Productions makes the “official” Bond films. The company was started in 1961 to make Dr No. Eon is trying hard to make Spectre another big success by keeping important people from Skyfall. Daniel Craig will play Bond at least until Bond 25, and the same writers wrote the script.

Many Bond fans are happy that the director Sam Mendes is back. Mendes is known for movies like American Beauty and Revolutionary Road, but he helped the Bond series reach a new high level. Gant says Mendes connected with both modern and traditional Bond fans. He also brought in famous actors like Ralph Fiennes. Big actors often want a director they respect.

Skyfall almost doubled the worldwide box office of the movie before it, Quantum of Solace. Quantum of Solace made $586m worldwide in 2008. No one expects Spectre to double Skyfall, but Eon still wants to do very well.

Before Mendes and Craig, the Bond series was not failing, but it started to feel a bit tired near the end of Pierce Brosnan’s time as Bond. The movies were no longer close to the original Ian Fleming books, and the series needed more energy.

The Bond series has always gone up and down. It often changes when a new actor plays Bond. The main Bond actors have been Sean Connery, George Lazenby, Roger Moore, Timothy Dalton, Pierce Brosnan, and now Daniel Craig. Some choices worked better than others.

Lazenby made only one Bond film. Dalton made two: The Living Daylights and Licence to Kill. These came out in the late 1980s, when other Hollywood action movies were very strong and exciting. Gant says audiences were not very excited by the last Roger Moore films and then Dalton’s films. Brosnan helped the series rise again in money made, and Craig has taken it to even higher levels. Gant also says the early Bond films were very successful, sexy, and exciting, and there were few movies like them at the time.

Even though Skyfall made more money than any other Bond movie, some older Bond films were almost as successful when you adjust for inflation. Thunderball (1965) is very close to Skyfall by that measure. Goldfinger and You Only Live Twice did better than the other Craig films. Two 1970s Bond films, The Spy Who Loved Me and Live and Let Die, also did better than the other Craig films. By this measure, Licence to Kill is the lowest-earning Bond film. Roger Moore’s last film, A View to a Kill, is second from the bottom.

Still, the Bond brand has stayed very strong for many years. Eon had to fight off other groups that wanted to use Bond’s popularity. Because of a strange situation with rights, other versions of Casino Royale (in 1967) and Thunderball (as Never Say Never Again, in 1983) were released to compete with Eon’s Bond films. After many legal fights over decades, Eon now fully controls both books.

The rights to Casino Royale were sold separately by Fleming in 1955. They finally went to Eon in 1999 because of an agreement between MGM (which supports Eon) and Sony. This allowed the 2006 version to be made. Thunderball had problems because writer-producer Kevin McClory helped Fleming plan the story and said he owned the novel. That led to McClory making Never Say Never Again. After McClory died in 2006, his family made a deal with MGM and Eon’s parent company in November 2013.

Bond movies were very popular in the 1960s and into the 1970s. But after Licence to Kill made little money, the series nearly stopped. Goldeneye, Brosnan’s first Bond film, brought the series back strongly. This was not only because of the new actor, but also because of the people making the movie.

After a six-year break, Eon chose director Martin Campbell. He was an experienced British director, and he created one of the most complex stunts in Bond history. In the famous opening of Goldeneye, Bond falls through the air into the cockpit of a small plane with no pilot. This helped make the series feel exciting and modern again. Also, the Goldeneye video game was very successful and reached a new generation. It helped people feel that Bond was not stale or old-fashioned anymore.

text:
Benjamin Carle is 26 years old. He tried to live for 10 months using only products made in France. This was for a TV documentary.

The idea came after a French minister, Arnaud Montebourg, told people to buy French products to help factories and jobs.

At the start, Carle learned something surprising: only 4.5% of the things in his apartment were made in France. Many things had to go, like his lightbulbs from China and green beans from Kenya. Moving workers took away most of his things, and his home was almost empty.

Carle had to give up many items because they were not French-made. He lost his smartphone, TV, and refrigerator (made in China). He also lost his glasses (Italian), underpants (Moroccan), coffee (Guatemalan), and his David Bowie music (British).

His girlfriend Anaïs and his cat Loon stayed with him.

Carle made three rules. He would eat only food produced in France. He would avoid foreign-made goods. And he would spend only €1,800 per month.

Life was hard. France does not make refrigerators, so he cooled food on the window ledge. He had no nail clippers, so he used a penknife on his toenails. He bought French clothes, but they cost more: underpants (€26), socks (€9), a polo shirt (€75), and espadrille sandals (€26). He could not buy jeans because none are made in France.

He searched in supermarkets for 100% French products. He learned to cook fruit and vegetables grown in France. He brushed his teeth with the last toothbrush made in France by a company in Picardie that employs 29 people. He washed small clothes by hand until he found the last French-made washing machine, but it was top-opening and did not fit under the kitchen counter.

Going out with friends was difficult. He could not watch American films. He could not drink Belgian beer. He could not eat sushi or pizza. At home, he had no sofa for the first few months and no TV. He listened to Michel Sardou and read French novels. French wine was allowed. French-Canadian singer Céline Dion was allowed too. But advisers said he should not listen to French bands like Daft Punk because they sing in English.

He could not use his British-made bicycle. He also could not use a French car after learning that the only affordable Peugeot, Renault, and Citroën models are mostly made overseas. So he bought an orange Mobylette moped that gave off bad fumes.

He replaced his computer with a Qooq, a cooking tablet that connects slowly to the internet. He replaced his iPhone with an old Sagem phone. In the documentary, he smashes his iPhone with a new French-made Tefal saucepan, and his girlfriend yells that the pans are new.

Carle said he wanted to “save the French economy,” and he joked that he likes Mission Impossible. The project was partly serious and partly a joke. He even asked a French language expert if he should use words like “cool.” He was told to use the French word “chouette” instead.

Carle learned France makes no refrigerators (except wine coolers) and no televisions, but it does make aeroplane seats and windmills. He said those things would not fit in his apartment.

At the end, Carle took a bank loan to buy furniture and clothes again. A special auditor said he was 96.9% “made in France.” Montebourg visited and gave him a medal. Carle was not 100% because of six Ikea forks, a guitar from China, and wall paint with no clear origin.

Carle said it is not fully possible, and not always good, to live 100% made in France, especially with new technology. He said the goal was not nationalism. He wanted people to think about what they buy and to make different choices, in every country. He said buying local can help jobs and industries.

He said a T-shirt made in France costs more, but he can feel sure workers are paid correctly and have good working conditions. He said he cannot be sure about a cheaper T-shirt made in Asia or Morocco. He also said it is hypocritical to blame capitalists for economic decline if people do not try to act differently as consumers.

Carle said he wants to keep supporting French producers, but not 100%, because finding everything takes too much time.

When the experiment ended, he invited his friends over. They ate cheese and listened to David Bowie’s album Aladdin Sane. He said it was hard not to invite people before because there was nowhere to sit, but he would choose Bowie over a sofa any day.

text:
He is not the first person to doubt Mars One. Mars One is a very big private plan to send people to live on Mars from 2025. But Joseph Roche is different from most critics because he is on the shortlist of possible astronauts.

Roche is an astrophysicist at Trinity College Dublin. In February, Mars One said he was one of 100 people still in line for the mission. He wrote in the Guardian that he has serious doubts that Mars One can work.

Roche says the selection process was not strict enough, compared with normal astronaut programs. He also says the Dutch Mars One team is being naïve if they think they can succeed on their own. The mission is supposed to cost $6bn, and he says it is now very unlikely to happen.

He says Mars One should be more open and transparent. He also says there are problems with the selection process, and that Mars One does not want to work with the scientific community. He thinks Mars One should admit this plan is not likely and instead support other space missions that are more realistic.

Roche also worries about how the organizers promoted a “top ten” list of candidates. He says this ranking did not mean these were the most likely astronauts. Instead, it was based on how many “supporter points” each person got, such as by buying official merchandise.

He says these “supporter points” show how much support someone gives to Mars One’s mission. He says the points do not affect the astronaut selection. They only show how much money supporters have donated to Mars One.

Mars One’s timeline says they plan to send a lander and a satellite to Mars in 2018, then a rover in 2020, and cargo missions starting in 2022. People would start arriving in 2025. Crews of four would go every two years to build the settlement. They would not return to Earth.

In February, a well-known supporter, Gerard ’t Hooft, a Dutch Nobel Prize winner in physics, said he did not think this schedule was realistic. He said it would take much longer and cost much more. He said that when they first asked him to join, he told them: “You have to put a zero after everything.”

Roche also spoke to Medium, a US blogging platform that has also shown strong doubt about Mars One. Medium reported that the claimed 200,000 astronaut applications were actually 2,761.

Roche explained the selection process to Medium. He said he has not met anyone from Mars One in person. At first, Mars One said there would be regional interviews, with travel, several days of interviews, and tests. He thought that sounded closer to a real astronaut selection.

But later, he said they made candidates sign a non-disclosure agreement to be interviewed. Then the plan changed from a multi-day regional interview to a ten-minute Skype call.

Roche told the Guardian he did not want to do more interviews because he did not want to seem too negative about space travel.

In his article, he said he loves pushing the limits of science, and that is why Mars One’s big idea attracted him. He said Mars One was never likely to overcome the money and technology problems in the time they planned. Still, he said it was interesting to hear a new idea that makes people think about the future of space exploration.

He also said being part of the public debate about the ethics and morality of future missions has been one of the most interesting and enjoyable parts of being a candidate. He said if a one-way mission to Mars ever became possible, he would volunteer. But he said this does not matter much, because he does not think we will see a one-way mission in his lifetime.

text:
Why do they do it? When you ask men who may be top referees in the future, they smile. This season, people have criticized referees more than before. Some former referees have even complained about the standards. This is surprising because referees often try to support each other.

So why do it? Why spend many hours driving around the country to enforce the rules? Some calls will upset people. Referees try to move up to higher levels, until they make decisions on TV in front of millions of viewers. People then watch them closely, using many camera angles and slow-motion replays.

One answer is seen on Lee Swabey’s face after he blows the final whistle in a match where Grimsby beat Woking 2–1. The match was at level 5 in the English league system. Swabey gets what referees hope for. “Twenty-two handshakes,” he says later, proudly. That means all the players shook his hand, and both managers said “well done.” For him, that is the best feeling. He loves the “buzz” of a game that goes smoothly. He says, “I wouldn’t spend so much time away from my family if this didn’t mean the world to me.”

Swabey is well thought of by Professional Game Match Officials Limited (PGMOL). He knew people were watching him at that match. PGMOL’s chief, Mike Riley, was there. Steve Dunn was also there. Dunn coaches referees at this level. They took notes and used a stopwatch to record important actions by the officials.

A few weeks earlier, Riley, Dunn, and another former referee, Peter Jones, went to another level-5 match. They watched another referee expected to move up: John Brooks. Brooks says, “I hope to have the opportunity to get promoted to the Premier League and officiate some of the top games in this country.”

But the main thing they saw was how Brooks handled a hard situation. The match had to be called off because the pitch was frozen. This is part of what Brooks must learn before he is trusted with bigger games. There are many problems a referee must deal with. Clubs often do not want a late postponement. They may still have to pay staff who came to work, but they will not earn any gate money.

Brooks called his coach for advice. He made a difficult but correct decision. Later, the club secretary arrived with envelopes to pay the officials for their time. At level 5, the match fee is £95, so it is clear these men do not do it for the money.

Brooks, like Swabey, wants to move up. He knows he must develop a thick skin and learn how to deal with disappointment. How does he feel when he watches TV and sees a referee attacked with angry comments? “Erm … not great,” he says. He remembers making a wrong decision once. The deciding goal was just offside. He says your heart sinks, and you cannot stop thinking about it.

He also says he wishes people understood how much time and effort referees put in. It is easy to criticize a decision, but referees do everything they can to get it right. Sometimes a referee will be unpopular. If you cannot handle that, he says, you are in the wrong job.

The former referees say today’s referees have much better support, education, and tools than they had. When Riley was a young referee, he bought books on psychology and nutrition because no one offered him information.

Brooks, in comparison, can phone his coach. They talk every week, discuss how games went, watch video of key decisions, and plan how to improve. Brooks also gets regular support from a sports psychologist, Liam Slack. He follows an exercise plan to help him cope with the 11km he runs during a game.

Brooks says psychology is very important. He says they talk about forgetting decisions and moving on. A big decision might happen in the first 30 seconds. After you decide, you must stay focused for the next 89 minutes. You cannot keep worrying about the earlier decision. Liam teaches techniques to let go of the decision. Brooks says this helps build mental toughness.

Jones says this modern approach can only help. He says that even when he refereed in professional football, he was really an amateur. He also worked for British Telecom. He might referee at Newcastle on a Wednesday evening and then be in Leicester at 9am the next morning talking to customers. He may not have slept. Training was not well planned. He says referees were amateurs in a professional world, compared to now.

When they talk about technology, the three former referees all support it. Riley says they favour anything that makes the referee’s job better and helps them be more effective on the field. The goal is to make fewer mistakes. Jones jokes that a bad decision can stay with you for a long time—“the rest of your life.”

text:
Scientists have made a mirror that can send heat from a building into cold space. They hope it could replace some air-conditioning on Earth.

Researchers think this mirror could greatly cut the energy used to keep places like offices and shopping centres cool. In the US, about 15% of building energy is used for air conditioning. The researchers’ calculations say that, in some cases, the mirror could remove the need for extra cooling.

In a test on a roof at Stanford, California, scientists compared different surfaces in sunlight. A black surface became 60C hotter than the air. Bare aluminium became 40C hotter than the air. But the mirror was up to 5C cooler than the air.

Shanhui Fan at Stanford University led the work. He said that if you cover a large part of a roof with the mirror, it could save a lot of power. In some situations, their calculations say it could completely offset air conditioning.

Buildings heat up in many ways. Boilers and cooking make heat. In hot countries, warm air enters through doors and windows. Sunlight and infrared heat from the sun also warm buildings.

The Stanford mirror reflects 97% of the visible light that hits it. More importantly, it also works like a heat radiator. When it warms up, it sends out heat as infrared light at a special wavelength. This infrared light can pass through the atmosphere and go into space.

To cool something, engineers say you need a “heat sink,” a place to put unwanted heat. The heat sink must be cooler than the thing you want to cool. For example, ice can cool a bottle of wine. The Stanford mirror uses the universe as the heat sink.

The mirror has several very thin layers. The first layer is reflective silver. On top are layers of silicon dioxide and hafnium oxide. These layers help it reflect light and also help it send out heat. When silicon dioxide heats up, it gives off infrared light at about ten micrometres. Very little in the air absorbs this wavelength, so the heat can go straight to space. The whole mirror is about two micrometres thick, or two thousandths of a millimetre.

The scientists wrote in Nature that the cold darkness of the universe can be used as a renewable resource for cooling, even in the hottest part of the day. In tests, the mirror had a cooling power of 40 watts per square metre at ambient temperature.

Fan wrote that installing the mirrors could cost between $20 and $70 per square metre. He also calculated a yearly electricity saving of 100MWh on a three-storey building.

Fan said the mirror could cool buildings, or other objects, by putting it in direct contact with them. Putting it only on a roof would block heating from sunlight, but it would not remove much heat from inside the building. He said it would more likely cool water or another fluid, and then that fluid could be pumped around the building.

He said the mirrors would not be a good way to slow global warming, because roof space is only a small part of Earth’s surface. He said their goal is to reduce electricity use.

Marin Solja__i__, a physicist at the Massachusetts Institute of Technology, said he is excited about the idea. He said it could be used on buildings so people spend much less on air conditioning, or maybe would not need it at all. He said putting it on top of shopping malls could give strong cooling if the surface area is large enough.

text:
The Moroccan city of Ouarzazate is used to big movie and TV projects. It is near the Sahara Desert. Many famous films and shows were filmed there, like Lawrence of Arabia, The Mummy, The Living Daylights, and Game of Thrones.

Now Ouarzazate, called the “door of the desert,” is the place for another very large project. It is building four connected solar power plants. Together with hydro power and wind power, these will help Morocco get nearly half of its electricity from renewable energy by 2020. Morocco also hopes to have extra electricity to sell to Europe. Morocco wants to use its deserts to become a world leader in solar power.

When the whole project is finished, it will be the largest concentrated solar power plant in the world. The first part, called Noor 1, will start working in November 2015. It uses mirrors, not the usual photovoltaic panels seen on many roofs. This mirror system costs more, but it can keep making electricity after the sun goes down.

People have known for many years that deserts have huge solar power potential. After the Chernobyl nuclear accident in 1986, German physicist Gerhard Knies said the world’s deserts get enough sun energy in a few hours to cover all human energy needs for a full year. The hard part is collecting this energy and moving it to the places where people live.

As engineers finish Noor 1, 500,000 crescent-shaped mirrors shine in the desert. There are 800 rows. The mirrors slowly follow the sun and move a little every few minutes.

When all four plants are done, they will cover an area as big as Rabat, Morocco’s capital. They will produce 580 mega-watts (MW) of electricity, enough for about one million homes. Noor 1 can produce 160MW.

Morocco’s Environment Minister, Hakima el-Haite, says solar energy could change the region in this century the way oil changed it in the last century. She said the $9bn project started because of urgent needs. Morocco does not produce oil. It imports 94% of its energy as fossil fuels from other countries, which hurts the state budget. Morocco also used to pay subsidies for fossil fuels, which cost a lot. So, when Morocco saw the chance to use solar energy, it decided to do it.

By 2020, solar energy will be one third of Morocco’s renewable energy. Wind and hydro will each be one third too.

El-Haite said Morocco is very proud of the project. She called it the most important solar plant in the world.

Each curved mirror is 12 metres high. It points sunlight at a steel pipe. The pipe carries a heat transfer solution (HTF), which is heated to 393C as it moves along the pipe and then goes into a heat engine. There, it is mixed with water to make steam. The steam turns turbines that make electricity.

The HTF is a synthetic thermal oil. It is pumped to a heat tank with molten sands that can store heat for three hours. This lets the plant power homes into the night. The mirrors are placed in levels to reduce damage from sand blown by desert winds.

Technicians say Noor 2 and Noor 3, planned to open in 2017, will store energy for up to eight hours. This could allow solar power all day and night in the Sahara and nearby areas.

Project director Rashid al-Bayad said the biggest challenge was finishing on time and reaching the needed performance level.

Morocco also has bigger international plans. Ahmed Baroudi, from Société d’Investissements Energétiques, said they are already working on high-voltage lines to cover southern Morocco and Mauritania as a first step. He said the final goal is much wider, even as far as the Middle East. He said the king’s final goal is Mecca.

It is not clear if this will happen. But Masen, Morocco’s solar energy agency, says exporting solar power could help make countries more stable. Morocco is talking with Tunisia, and sending energy across the Mediterranean to the north is still an important goal.

Masen spokeswoman Maha el-Kadiri said exporting to Europe could be possible, but first they would need to build new interconnectors, because they do not exist yet. She said these connections would not use the existing one in Spain. After building them, Morocco could start exporting.

Spain has stopped new solar projects because it does not have enough interconnectors to send energy to France. The EU has a goal that by 2020, 10% of each member country’s electricity can be sent abroad by cable.

For now, Morocco is focused on using solar power to meet its own needs and be more independent. In the future, this could include desalination, to make fresh water from sea water. Morocco is being hit more often by drought as the climate warms. Officials know Morocco is leading the most advanced renewable energy program in the Middle East and North Africa region. El-Kadiri said Morocco is at the front of solar energy.

About $9bn has been invested in the Noor Complex. Much of it came from international groups like the European Investment Bank and the World Bank, with support from Moroccan government guarantees. The cost has not been passed on to energy customers because of undisclosed energy subsidies from King Mohammed VI, Morocco’s unelected ruler.

One month before Noor 1 starts, more than a thousand workers, mostly Moroccan, are still working fast. They are fixing electric wires, removing scaffolding, and wrapping insulation around steel pipes. They work 12-hour shifts with the Atlas Mountains behind them. They wear hard hats, safety shoes, and ear plugs.

Hajar Lakhael is 25 years old and works as an environment and security manager from Meknes. She says the preparation is almost finished.

text:
Music subscription services, like Spotify and Deezer, have made over $1 billion in sales worldwide. More and more fans are choosing to pay for music online.

In 2013, money from streaming and subscriptions rose by more than 50% and reached $1.1 billion. Figures published in March 2014 said this helped recorded music sales in Europe grow for the first time in 12 years.

There are about 450 music-rental services around the world. Many people still listen for free, but more people are paying because they want more choice. In three years, the number of paying subscribers grew from 8 million to 28 million. This information came from the 2014 digital music report from the International Federation of the Phonographic Industry (IFPI).

People can use these services easily on smartphones and tablets. They are popular because people can try new music without buying a download or a CD. Consumers say they like that it is cheap, easy to use, and legal instead of pirated downloads. IFPI Chief Executive Frances Moore said music streaming and subscription is now a main way of doing business.

The IFPI also said One Direction were the biggest-selling artists of 2013. They had 4 million physical and digital sales for their album Midnight Memories. Katy Perry’s Prism was the best-selling album by a female artist. It was in sixth place, behind Eminem, Justin Timberlake, Bruno Mars, and Daft Punk.

Technology companies are also joining music streaming. Apple launched iTunes Radio, and Google promoted its Play Store. Smaller companies like Beats Music, made by the team behind the Beats headphones brand, also joined.

In Britain and America, streaming may soon bring in more money for the music industry than downloads from online stores like Apple’s iTunes. Subscription services are now one third of all digital sales worldwide. Downloads make up the rest, but they are growing at different speeds.

In the US, the percent of people who said they use subscription and streaming went from 19% in 2012 to 23%. The percent of people downloading went from 28% to 27%. In Britain, the number of downloaders stayed the same at exactly one third, but subscribers grew from 19% to 22%. In Sweden, France, and Italy, streaming is already more popular than downloading.

Digital formats now make up 39% of all music sales, or nearly £5.9 billion out of £15 billion. CD and vinyl sales fell a lot in 2013, but they still bring in just over half of the industry’s income. Music sales in Japan dropped. Japan makes up one fifth of music industry sales, and physical formats stayed popular there longer than in other places. Because of this, total global sales across all formats fell 3.9%.

However, vinyl is coming back in some markets. Vinyl sales rose by 32% in America and by 101% in the UK in 2013.

text:
The customer next to you in the line looks normal. But she is not holding a shopping list. She has handwritten notes about how the store looks and how clean it is. She is timing how fast the line moves on her phone. You may even see a tiny camera lens in her purse. You may have seen a mystery shopper.

In the UK, about 50,000 mystery shopping trips happen every month, according to the Mystery Shopping Providers Association. More people now shop online, but the need for mystery shoppers is still growing. Simon Boydell from Marketforce says people who come into a real store want good service and a good experience that they cannot get online. Companies want to check if their stores give that experience.

Jill Spencer from the mystery shopping company ABa says they send shoppers to different stores and change the locations, so a shopper does not return to the same store within three months. Each day, shoppers may spend up to eight hours visiting five to ten stores. Then they spend another one or two hours writing detailed reports about every part of the visit. They can earn up to £155 a day. They also get money back for petrol and hotel stays, and money for car wear (they may drive up to 20,000 miles a year). Video mystery shoppers, who film visits with a hidden camera in a buttonhole or handbag, can earn more, about £300 a day.

Mystery shoppers are usually paid back for what they buy in the store. They may also be allowed to keep the items. Laura is a 50-year-old mystery shopper from Devon. She says she is usually given £5 to £20 to spend in each store to test the service at the till. She has been paid to visit about 7,000 shops since 2001. She is given a story to follow, like buying something from a certain department or a new product range. But within those rules, she can often buy what she wants and keep it.

Like many full-time mystery shoppers, Laura is self-employed. She takes jobs from ABa and other companies when they are available. She usually earns £30,000 to £40,000 a year, not counting the free items she gets. She says the extra benefits help her live. But she says she does not do it because she loves shopping. She says she now hates shopping, and it even hurts to buy a pint of milk when she is not working.

Laura does like it when she visits a store again and sees it has improved. She thinks this is because of her feedback. She says some stores win awards for customer service, and she thinks mystery shoppers help with that. She feels she is helping all shoppers.

But jobs that pay like Laura’s are not common. There is strong competition. She keeps her job secret, and even her friends and family do not know who she works for. Laura is not her real name.

It is estimated that more than 500,000 people have registered as mystery shoppers in the UK, but only 10% or less get regular work each month. Because of this, pay has gone down. Val, a 51-year-old former mystery shopper, says that in the past you got a fee, your purchase paid back, and money for miles. Now you often get only part of the cost of a purchase. Val worked for 40 different mystery shopping companies for almost 20 years. She stopped three years ago because she had bills to pay and very few jobs paid an acceptable amount.

Today, mystery shopping companies often use free items to attract workers. Boydell says Marketforce shoppers usually get only a couple of pounds for a visit as a small payment for their time. At most, they may pay £15 to £25, plus paying back the cost of something like a meal for two or a hotel stay. He says they do not directly employ shoppers, so they do not have to pay minimum wage.

Laura says she would go on a cruise for free, but she thinks some companies take advantage of people when they pay only a small fee for a job that needs travel to a restaurant and a meal. She says she does not do those jobs anymore. But many people still do. Hannah is a 41-year-old City lawyer. In her spare time, she has done nearly 500 visits for the Mystery Dining Company without pay or travel money. As a “platinum diner,” she is often chosen for the most special jobs. She gets £200 meals at Michelin-starred Mayfair restaurants, five-star hospitality at Ascot, and overnight stays at boutique hotels.

But Hannah says nothing is truly free. After each visit, she usually spends two to four hours writing detailed reports. She writes about the food and about specific moments with staff. She must name staff or describe them in a “subtle” way. The hardest part is that she must remember everything while she eats, because she cannot openly write notes.

Hannah says there is a lot to remember, and it can take away from the meal. She is expected to write feedback while it is fresh, so she has had to wake up at 5am to write a report before work. She says it is hard work, and you must be focused, able to write well, and good with details.

text:
Astronaut Scott Kelly told reporters how he stayed calm during 340 days in space. He said he looked at Himalayan lakes, did spacewalks, and followed the US presidential primaries. After he landed back on Earth from a record-setting mission, he said, “It seemed like I lived there forever.” He said the biggest surprise was how long it felt. He also said that sometimes you “go bananas.”

Kelly and a Russian astronaut, Mikhail Kornienko, spent almost a year on the International Space Station (ISS). They did this to study how weightlessness, radiation, and small living spaces affect people. NASA says this research is needed for a future mission to Mars.

Kelly said the hardest part was the long time in space. He said he felt much more sore when he came back to Earth than after shorter trips. Kelly and his twin brother, Mark, who is a retired astronaut, have been doing physical and mental tests for the last year. The tests will continue so NASA can learn how the body handles the stress of space flight.

Kelly said he had aches and very sensitive skin. So far, he said his balance has been mostly okay. But he said the first thing he tried to throw on a table, he missed, because you can underestimate gravity.

He said coming back to gravity was uncomfortable. In space, he grew 1.5 inches taller, and then returned to his normal height on Earth. Still, he said he felt amazed after his capsule landed. When the Russian capsule opened in the cool air of Kazakhstan, he said he smelled a fragrance like a plant was blooming. He said this fresh air mixed with the burned, kind of sweet smell of the spacecraft after it came back through the atmosphere.

As he moved away from the capsule, he said he started to understand what the mission meant: 340 days on a 15-year-old space station. He said the ISS is a million pounds and the size of a football field. He said the inside space is, some say, like a six-bedroom house. He said the ISS uses power from the sun and was built with help from an international team and millions of taxpayers.

Kelly said people will discover new things from life and work on the space station that they do not even know yet. He compared the research from more than 450 missions to the work done by NASA computer scientists in the 1960s and 1970s. He also said, “The view is great, too.”

Kelly shared many photos online of Earth’s cities, land, oceans, and atmosphere. He said, “The Earth is a beautiful planet.” He described the bright colors near the Bahamas and the rainbow colors of lakes in the northern Himalayas. He said he would like to visit that area, but first he would need to learn what country owns the lakes. He said you mostly notice how thin and fragile the atmosphere looks. He said that, along with large areas of pollution, is alarming.

He said he could see pollution systems from space. He could see smoke clouds from wildfires over parts of the US. He could also see parts of Asia with pollution that was visible almost all year. He said the message “we need to save the planet” misses the point. He said the planet will get better, but humans might not be here because humans could destroy the environment. He said seeing the thin atmosphere can make you more of an environmentalist.

He said people need to take care of the air they breathe and the water they drink. He said he believes people affect this and can change it if they decide to.

Kelly said his social media work was done together with his girlfriend, and it gave him many followers. But he said he did not know much about his popularity. Instead, he watched the 2016 presidential election news. One of the first questions he asked the crew that helped him out of the capsule was, “How did Super Tuesday go?”

But he did not share his opinions about Donald Trump and Bernie Sanders. He said that as a government employee he must follow the Hatch Act, a law that stops some political activity by federal employees.

He said doing steady work helped him stay sane. He said he set small goals, like the next crew arriving, the next spacewalk, the next robotics work, and the next science experiment. He said that helped his sanity. He said being back on Earth with everyone had not fully felt real yet. He remembered being shocked to see a crowd after a past mission. He said he might feel culture shock soon.

A NASA scientist said Kelly showed the qualities needed for a mission to Mars. But Kelly said he probably will not fly again with NASA. Still, he said he would not say he is 100% done. He mentioned the success of private spaceflight companies like SpaceX. He said they might need someone like him someday. He also said that maybe in the next 20 years, people could buy a cheap ticket and go for a short visit to space.

text:
Scientists have made an “atlas of the brain.” It shows how the meanings of words are spread across different parts of the brain. The atlas looks like a colorful quilt on the brain’s surface. It shows groups of related words in small areas.

Jack Gallant, a brain scientist at the University of California, Berkeley, said their goal was to build a big map that shows how the brain represents word meaning.

No single place in the brain stores just one word or idea. One small brain area can link to many related words. Also, one word can use many different brain areas. These areas work together as networks for meanings, like “life” and “love,” “death” and “taxes,” “clouds,” “Florida,” and “bra.”

One researcher who did not help with the study called the atlas a “tour de force.” The atlas shows how new brain-imaging tools can change what we know about how the brain works. In the future, this technology could help medicine and other fields.

Alexander Huth, the first author of the study, said this method might help decode what words a person is hearing, reading, or maybe even thinking. One possible use is a language decoder. It could help people who cannot speak because of motor neurone disease or locked-in syndrome communicate through a computer.

To build the atlas, the scientists recorded brain activity while people listened to stories. The stories were read from The Moth Radio Hour, a radio show in the United States. The scientists matched the story text with the brain data. They showed that groups of related words caused responses in about 50,000 to 80,000 pea-sized spots across the cerebral cortex.

Huth used The Moth Radio Hour because the stories are short and interesting. If the stories are interesting, people are more likely to pay attention. Seven people listened to two hours of stories each while lying in a scanner. Each person heard about 25,000 words, including more than 3,000 different words.

The atlas shows that related words often use the same brain areas. For example, on the left side of the brain above the ear, one tiny area responds to the word “victim.” The same area also responds to “killed,” “convicted,” “murdered,” and “confessed.” On the right side of the brain near the top of the head, one area responds to family words like “wife,” “husband,” “children,” and “parents.”

A word is shown in more than one brain spot because words can have more than one meaning. One brain area responds to the word “top” with other clothing words. But “top” also activates other areas. One responds to numbers and measurements, and another to buildings and places. The scientists also made an interactive website so the public can explore the atlas.

The atlases looked similar for all the people in the study. This suggests their brains organized word meanings in a similar way. But the scientists only scanned five men and two women. All were native English speakers, and two were authors of the Nature paper. People from other backgrounds and cultures may have different brain atlases for meaning.

With this atlas, researchers can study brain networks for very different ideas, from numbers to murder and religion. Gallant said the idea of murder is represented a lot in the brain.

Using the same data, the team has started new atlases for other parts of language, from phonemes to syntax. But an atlas for story structure has been hard to make. Gallant said that when they choose story features, people tell them the features are not the right ones.

Uri Hasson, a neuroscientist at Princeton University, praised the work. He said it showed how the brain works in a real-world situation, not just with one word or one sentence. He said the next step is a bigger and more exact atlas. Hasson believes it may one day be possible to rebuild the words a person is thinking from brain activity. This raises huge ethical questions. A less harmful use could be checking if political messages were understood by the public.

Lorraine Tyler, a cognitive neuroscientist at the University of Cambridge, also called the work a “tour de force.” But she said the atlas does not yet show small differences in word meanings. She said this research is important, but there is still a lot to learn about how the brain represents word meaning.
