text:
New research says noise from passing ships may bother killer whales and dolphins more than people thought. The noise can make it harder for them to talk to each other and to find food. People have known that the low rumble of ships can disturb large whales. But US researchers also found strong ship noise at medium and high pitches. This includes 20,000Hz, the pitch where killer whales, also called orcas, hear best.

This noise may make it harder for killer whales to communicate and to use echolocation. Echolocation is when an animal uses sound to bounce off objects, like prey, to find where they are. Dolphins and porpoises may have the same problem because they also use high pitches.

The findings may matter a lot for the endangered killer whales that live near shipping lanes. Only 84 of these killer whales feed along the US west coast and into Puget Sound.

Scott Veirs of Beamreach led the research. He said even a small increase in noise can make echolocation harder for whales. This is a worry because their prey, chinook salmon, is already scarce. He said hearing a small click from a salmon is very hard, and it is even harder when there is a lot of noise.

The researchers used underwater microphones to measure noise from about 1,600 ships as they passed through Haro Strait in Washington State. The study lasted two years. It recorded sounds from 12 kinds of vessels, including cruise ships, container ships, and military vehicles. About 20 ships a day passed through the strait.

Some ships were quieter than others. On average, the noise next to the ships was 173 underwater decibels. That is like 111 decibels in air, about as loud as a loud rock concert. Whales are usually not right next to ships. They would more likely hear about 60 to 90 decibels, like a lawnmower or a vacuum cleaner.

Veirs said scientists already knew underwater noise affects baleen whales, which include the largest animals on Earth. But this study shows a threat to smaller whales, dolphins, and porpoises too. He said ships were thought to mostly make low-pitch noise, like lorries or trains. Most noise is low, but ship noise can also raise the background noise of the ocean at high pitches. This could be a serious problem that needs more study.

A noisy ocean can cause other problems. Whales may need to swim closer together so they can hear each other. If they cannot find prey as well, they may need to use up extra blubber. This is a problem because blubber can hold manmade pollutants. These pollutants can be toxic to whales if they are released into the body.

Veirs said more work is needed to learn how much the noise is hurting whales and how to make ships quieter near these animals. He said it should be easy to reduce noise pollution. He said military ships are much quieter, and their technology could be used for commercial ships. Another way is to slow down. He said dropping speed by six knots could cut noise intensity by half.

Some whale species, like humpbacks and blue whales, have done better as whaling has gone down. But other whales are still in danger for many reasons. The US federal government recently protected nearly 40,000 square miles of the Atlantic to help avoid losing the critically endangered North Atlantic right whale, which has only about 500 individuals left.

In Europe, killer whales have dangerously high levels of banned PCB chemicals in their blubber. Scientists are still trying to find out if pollutants caused the deaths of five sperm whales that stranded on the east coast of Britain in January 2016. Around the coast of Australia, whales face more danger from ship strikes and oil and gas drilling. Japan has also pledged to resume whaling in Antarctic waters.

text:
Researchers were shocked by how far people would go to avoid a simple task. The task was to sit in a chair and do nothing but think.

Some people found this so boring that they chose to give themselves mild electric shocks just to stop the boredom.

In one test, men and women sat alone for 15 minutes. Two-thirds of the men pressed a button to give themselves a painful shock. One-quarter of the women did the same. Scientists think this may be because men often look for stronger feelings than women.

Psychologists from Virginia and Harvard Universities did more than 11 studies. They found that people did not like being left alone with their thoughts. This was true no matter their age, education, income, or how much they used smartphones or social media.

Timothy Wilson, who led the research, said the results may not be because modern life is so busy. Instead, people may like phones and social media because they feel a strong need to do something rather than do nothing.

In the first experiments, students were led into a plain room alone. They had no phones, no books, and nothing to write with. They had to stay seated and not fall asleep. They were told they would be alone for six to 15 minutes.

After the time ended, the students said they did not enjoy it. They had trouble focusing. Their minds wandered even though nothing was distracting them. Even when they were given time to plan what to think about, it did not help.

The researchers then tried the experiment at people’s homes, in case the room was the problem. The results were similar, but people felt even more miserable. Some cheated by standing up or checking their phones.

To see if this was only a student problem, the scientists recruited more than 100 people, ages 18 to 77, from a church and a farmers’ market. They also disliked being alone with their thoughts.

Then came the most surprising result. The researchers wanted to see if people would choose something bad instead of doing nothing. They gave students the choice to give themselves a mild electric shock.

Earlier, students had rated how unpleasant the shocks were. They also rated other unpleasant things, like looking at pictures of cockroaches or hearing the sound of a knife rubbing against a bottle.

After trying a shock, every student said they would pay money to avoid mild electric shocks.

But in the test, 12 of 18 men gave themselves up to four shocks. Six of 24 women did too.

The scientists wrote in the journal Science that being alone with their thoughts seemed so unpleasant that it made many people shock themselves, even though they had said they would pay to avoid it.

Jessica Andrews-Hanna at the University of Colorado said some students might shock themselves to make a boring lecture feel better. But she said we need to know more about why people pressed the button in this study.

She asked us to imagine the setup: a person sits in a chair with wires on their skin and a button that gives a harmless but uncomfortable shock. They are told to sit and entertain themselves with their thoughts.

As time passes, she said, the person’s mind may wander to the shock and wonder: was it really that bad? She also suggested the person may be curious about what the researchers really want to learn. She said it might be a case where curiosity caused the problem.

text:
In stores, cameras watch you. If you pick up something like a boot, a camera can make sure you do not hide it in your bag. But new technology is making stores watch different things. They are less focused on stealing and more interested in your age, sex, size, and what you look like.

A few months ago, the IT firm Computer Sciences Corporation (CSC) wrote a report. It said about 30% of retailers use facial recognition to track customers inside stores. Facial recognition can identify people by studying faces and comparing them to a database. It can use devices like Intel RealSense cameras. These cameras can study facial expressions and even the clothing brands someone is wearing.

Joe Jensen from Intel’s Retail Solutions Division says the goal is not to build databases about specific people. He says the goal is to build general models about people’s lifestyles and shopping habits. He says shops do not need to know one exact customer. They want to know the kind of shopper a person is, and what people like that have done in the past.

If stores combine recognition technology with past customer data, they can guess what a person might do in a shop. For example, if a size-10 woman wearing a gold necklace walks quickly toward the sock aisle, a store might guess she wants to buy socks. Then the store could show ads on screens aimed at her. If she looks like someone who wants socks, it will show sock ads.

This may sound familiar because the internet has used these ideas for years. If you search for something on Amazon, you may see ads for similar products on other sites. If you show interest in canoeing, you may see canoe ads in many places. But using these systems in real stores is not as easy as copying what happens online. People do not react to cameras the same way they react to browser cookies.

Hoxton Analytics, a team of data scientists in London, made a system using machine learning and artificial intelligence. It groups people by the shoes they wear. By looking at the style and size of shoes as people walk past a sensor, the system can guess a customer’s gender with 75 to 80% accuracy.

Owen McCormack, the CEO of Hoxton Analytics, says the system was partly a response to facial recognition. He asked why not use clothes to understand basic information about people. He said if you saw a picture of someone’s body, you could probably guess their gender. But aiming a camera at someone’s chest or hips can feel as creepy as facial recognition. So they focused on shoes instead.

The word “creepy” is often used when people talk about tracking customers in stores. Retailers and data scientists want to get information without seeming too intrusive.

For Hoxton Analytics and the stores using it, the answer is to look down at shoes. This shows how people react to being watched in real life. It suggests there are limits that do not exist on the internet. If a camera aims at our bodies, we can feel invaded. But it is not clear if judging people by their shoes is really less invasive.

McCormack says the key point is that they are not collecting personally identifiable information. He says shops already do many invasive things, but people may not know about them. He says if a shop knows if someone is male or female, ads can be more efficient. If everyone in the shop is male, the shop might advertise PlayStations instead of hairdryers.

Hidden monitoring can help shops improve results while customers do not notice. But if stores add screens that change and show targeted ads, it is harder to hide that a machine is watching you. Retailers say they do this to give a personal shopping experience, but it is still a grey area. Many people still think it feels creepy.

Retailers understand that physical shops want some of the same information that online stores get. People allow this online, so why not offline? But the rules feel different in a mall than between websites. In a mall you can walk freely without a pop-up asking you to accept cookies, so consent works differently.

Younger people may feel less bothered because they grew up with online life first and physical shops second. The CSC report said 72% of people aged 55 and over were very uncomfortable with these technologies in physical shops. Only 51% of 16 to 24 year olds said the same.

Is this because younger people know digital technology better, or because they trust big organizations that offer free services? Is a technology always creepy in the same way, or does it change as society accepts it? Either way, more and more “eyes” are watching between the store shelves, and they care a lot about what you are wearing.

text:
A new report says the average six-year-old understands more about digital technology than a 45-year-old adult. Many children may not know who Steve Jobs was, and some cannot tie their shoelaces, but they still know a lot about devices.

Ofcom, a group that checks standards in the UK communications industries, says broadband in 2000 helped create a generation of “digital natives.” These children were born in the new millennium. They never used slow dial-up internet. Some learn to use smartphones or tablets before they can talk.

Jane Rumble, Ofcom’s media research head, said young people are changing the way we communicate. She said they have different habits from older people, even compared with “early adopters” aged 16 to 24.

Ofcom made a “digital quotient” (DQ) test. They tested 800 children and 2,000 adults. The test is not like an IQ test. It checks how well people understand and feel confident using gadgets like tablets and smart watches. It also checks knowledge of superfast internet, 4G mobile-phone networks, and mobile apps.

Children aged 6 to 7 scored an average DQ of 98. Adults aged 45 to 49 scored 96. Digital understanding is highest at ages 14 to 15, with an average DQ of 113. After that, it slowly drops in adulthood and drops faster in old age.

People can now take a shorter version of the test. It gives them a DQ score and advice on how to improve and stay safe online.

Experts say millennial children communicate and enjoy entertainment in very different ways from older generations. Forecasters think these children’s choices may show the future better than the choices of trendsetting young adults.

The biggest change is phone calls. Twenty years ago, teenagers spent hours at night talking on the home phone.

Now, for ages 12 to 15, phone calls are only 3% of their communication time on any device. For all adults, phone calls are 20%. For young adults, they are 9%. Children mostly socialize from far away by sending written messages and sharing photos and videos. Ofcom says the millennium generation is “losing its voice.”

Over 90% of children’s device time is messaging. They chat on social networks like Facebook, send instant messages with services like WhatsApp, or use traditional text messages. Only 2% of children’s time is email, compared with 33% for adults.

Children aged 12 to 15 also use other media differently. A seven-day digital diary shows that live TV is only about half of what they watch. For all adults, live TV is nearly 70%. Children spend 20% of viewing time on short video clips, such as on YouTube, or news clips shared on Facebook and other social sites. The rest is DVDs, streamed content through Netflix or iTunes, and recorded TV programmes.

Young adults aged 16 to 24 use a lot of different media. But live radio and print media are almost gone from what they use each day.

Younger people are moving from live TV to streaming and catch-up services. Even for adults, TV is becoming less important. TV viewing for 16 to 24-year-olds has gone down each year since 2010. In 2013, viewing fell across all age groups for the first time. Researchers think tablets helped, because many older people used the internet more. Tablets have big screens and simple touch controls. They are linked to more internet access for people over 65.

James Thickett, Research Director at Ofcom, said people over 65 resisted using the internet for years. He said this changed in the last three years, and Ofcom thinks tablets are the reason.

In Britain, people of all ages are using internet devices more. Screen time is now more than sleep time. The typical adult spends 8 hours and 41 minutes a day communicating or using media, including books and newspapers, and sleeps 8 hours and 21 minutes.

text:
People call Enric Duran the “Robin Hood of the banks.” He took many bank loans, almost 500,000 euros in total. He did not plan to pay the money back. Instead, he gave the money to projects that supported alternatives to capitalism.

Duran hid for 14 months. He says he is not sorry, even though he could go to jail. In a Skype interview from a secret place, he said, “I’m proud of what I did.” He said the money created chances for people. He said it helped start a movement and build a strong network of groups working on these ideas.

From 2006 to 2008, Duran took 68 business and personal loans from 39 banks in Spain. He gave the money to social activists. He paid for talks against capitalism and for TV cameras for a media network. He said these social movements were building new ways to live, but they did not have enough money or good ways to communicate. He also said the economic system depended on constant growth and created money “out of nothing.”

He said taking the loans was his way to challenge and speak against this situation. He started slowly. He first filled out some loan forms using his real information. The banks said no, but he wanted to learn what they asked for.

Later, the former table-tennis coach made a complicated system of accounts, payments, and transfers. He said he was always learning. By summer 2007, he learned how to make it work. He applied for loans using the name of a fake TV production company. He said he then got a lot of money: 492,000 euros.

In 2009, Duran was arrested in Spain. Six of the 39 banks made charges against him. He spent two months in prison. Then he got out after paying bail of 50,000 euros. In February 2013, he faced up to eight years in prison. He decided to run away instead of going to trial. He said he did not see the court system as legitimate because he did not accept its authority.

He said his actions were part of a worldwide debate about the economic crisis. The timing helped the anti-capitalist movement get more attention. Many people in Spain were looking for other ways because the system had badly harmed their lives.

He said people in Spain today might understand his actions better. But he also said they would not be needed now. He said the anti-capitalist movement has grown from a small group to one supported by thousands of Spaniards. He pointed to popular movements like the Indignados.

Duran said the movement has become able to support itself. “We now have the capacity to generate resources,” he said. He added, in an ironic way, that this is what banks give credit for: to help you move forward and become independent. Duran is also focusing on Spain’s justice system by supporting restorative justice. He said some people in Spain believe banks do not work and think he does not owe anything. He said, “I’ve already done my work.” But he also said some people do not agree with him, and he thinks he should answer them.

He said he might offer something back to banks in a different way. He said he learned a lot from getting loans by fraud. He said he could share ideas about what works and what does not work, for the general public and for bank workers.

text:
The view from the visitors’ centre on the south edge of Donana National Park in southern Spain is amazing. It is a great place for people who study birds. The park has 200,000 hectares of salt marsh, and it is very important for birds in western Europe. Many birds from Britain stop here each year when they travel from Africa. These include house martins, swallows, cuckoos, and warblers. Donana is a UN World Heritage Site. It also has very rare birds like the Spanish imperial eagle. It also has mammals like the Iberian lynx, which is very endangered.

Donana is beautiful, but it is also in danger. In 1998, a dam at Los Frailes mine, 45 km to the north, broke. Almost two billion gallons of polluted, very acidic water mixed with waste metals flowed into the park. The poison rushed down the Guadiamar river and over the river banks. It left a thick metal layer over a large area. After this, workers collected more than 25,000 kilos of dead fish. Nearly 2,000 adult birds, chicks, eggs, and nests were killed or destroyed.

This was Spain’s worst environmental disaster. Cleaning it up cost 90m. Spain then decided to spend another 360m, including some EU money, to restore the land. In the 1950s and 60s, some places had been drained to make rice and cotton fields. Now some of this farmland is being changed back into wetland.

This work has been expensive, but it has helped. Still, Donana is not safe. Modern life brings more pressure. There are plans for an oil pipeline through Donana. Other developers want to grow nearby tourist resorts. New hotels and golf courses would need a lot of water, which could lower the local water table. Also, silt from nearby farms is filling up the channels that run across Donana.

A big worry for people who protect nature is a new decision by the Andalucian government. They want to reopen the Frailes mine, the mine that almost destroyed Donana in 1998. Laurence Rose of the RSPB says Donana is Europe’s most precious bird sanctuary. It is important for local bird species and also for birds that travel between Africa and Britain and other parts of north-west Europe. He says Donana already has many threats, and bringing back the cause of the old disaster is extremely worrying.

It may seem strange to reopen the mine after spending so much to repair Donana. But the local economy helps explain it. Spain’s bank crash in 2008 badly hurt the region. Unemployment in some parts of Andalucia is now more than 30%. Reopening the mine could provide more than 1,000 jobs.

Vicente Fernandez Guerrero, secretary-general of Innovation, Industry and Energy for Andalucia, said there are riches there that local people need. He said mining could help local people keep living in the area. He also said this is a mining area and people have dug metals and ores there since Roman times.

Fernandez also said the mine licence would require modern mining methods. These methods would avoid making poisonous wet waste. The government has also said the Canadian company that ran Los Frailes cannot bid for the contract. Sixteen years after the accident, the two sides still disagree about compensation. Fernandez insisted that the best technology will be used and that liquid will not be used.

Some people in the area support the plan, but many oppose it. One road sign had rude words about the workers’ union UGT, which supports reopening the mine. Carlos Davila, from the Spanish Ornithological Society in Donana, was also very worried. He said it is a very bad idea. He said people also said it was safe in 1998, but it caused Spain’s worst ecological disaster.

Davila and others worry that a new mine could harm eco-tourism in Donana. In recent years, people have invested a lot in nature tourism there. For example, at the restaurant Dehesa de Abajo, visitors can eat and drink among trees where storks and black kites nest.

Almost every visitor had a camera with a long lens or binoculars. People can make money from tourists who come to see the birds of Donana. This is not surprising because it is a special place. A huge sky stretches over the flat land. Birds of many kinds fill the air and sometimes stand on the road. During the visit, a stork stood calmly in front of the car until it decided to fly away.

The problem is that Spain does not have the same public resources it had 16 years ago. Rose said that if another toxic spill happened today, it would cause much more damage. Davila agreed. He said that after the disaster, Spain understood how important Donana was and worked hard to clean it up and protect it. He said that now people seem to be forgetting that lesson, and it is very depressing.

text:
A long time ago, people went to the movies and saw Star Wars. It showed a galaxy far away. Big ships fought in space. There were droids and Wookiees. A ship called the Millennium Falcon could make the Kessel Run in less than 12 parsecs.

That was in 1977. In 2015, Star Wars was still very popular. There was news about a new movie about Han Solo. There were also rumors that Darth Vader might appear again.

Paul Dergarabedian, a media analyst at Rentrak, said many fans around the world keep watching for new posters, new trailers, and other news. He said it is hard to think of another movie series that makes people feel so excited.

The newest big wave of Star Wars excitement started after Disney bought Lucasfilm from George Lucas in 2012. Disney paid $4 billion. Disney also said there would be three more episodes: VII, VIII, and IX. They also planned spin-off movies.

Now there are details about the second spin-off movie. It will tell the early story of Han Solo. Harrison Ford played Han Solo in the first three movies. The Han Solo movie is planned for May 2018.

Before that, Episode VII will come out in December 2015. JJ Abrams directed it. It is called Star Wars: The Force Awakens. Episode VIII is planned for 2017. Another spin-off movie, Rogue One, is planned for 2016.

Rogue One will tell about a rebel mission to steal the plans for the Death Star. This is important in the first movie from 1977. People were also very interested in rumors that Rogue One might include Darth Vader. This showed how strong Star Wars memories are for fans.

Disney and Lucasfilm are making many movies in one Star Wars “universe,” with many stories and characters. They are doing something like Marvel Studios, which Disney bought in 2009.

Disney also uses strong marketing. Dergarabedian said it was a great idea to put all six older Star Wars movies on streaming services. He said this helps build excitement for the new movie and helps fans think about Star Wars again.

Some people think Star Wars does not need help to stay popular. For at least the last 20 years, fans have followed the “Expanded Universe.” This includes many novels, comic books, video games, and merchandise.

This extra material started in 1978. It grew much more in the 1990s as the internet became bigger. Fans still enjoy role-play and events. In the UK, Secret Cinema has made money by putting on a live show of The Empire Strikes Back.

Michael Rosser, news editor for Screen International, said this shared universe and strong nostalgia keep Star Wars at the top. He said the original movies made a huge world of characters and ideas.

Rosser said that for years people have wondered how the different story parts would connect. He said the new movie, because it goes back to characters like Han Solo and Luke Skywalker, looks like it will connect to the original Star Wars better than the prequels did.

Rosser meant the three movies George Lucas made from 1999 to 2005: The Phantom Menace, Attack of the Clones, and Revenge of the Sith. These movies told the story of Luke Skywalker’s father, Anakin, who becomes Darth Vader. The reviews were not very strong, but the prequels still made $2.5 billion worldwide. Before that, “special editions” of the first three movies made $469 million in 1997.

Rosser said this shows the power of Star Wars. Even when people were disappointed, the movies still made a lot of money.

The article asks if there is a risk that movie studios will only care about brands and not about movies as art.

Rosser said he does not think so. He said they want the Star Wars series to last a long time and to keep the quality high. He also said they want people to go to movie theaters, when many people stay home for entertainment. He said you do not want to watch Star Wars on an iPhone, so he does not think it will lose power soon.

Dergarabedian expects The Force Awakens to make a lot of money when it comes out in December 2015. He said it could have a record opening for December and could make at least $1 billion worldwide. He said Star Wars is the biggest movie brand.

text:
We are not living in a time of flying cars like in the 1985 film Back to the Future II. But smartphones and other new technology are changing travel in ways that can feel just as amazing. Experts say that changes in the economy and population, new technology, and worries about the environment are changing transportation in a big way. Robert Puentes from the Brookings Institution’s Metropolitan Policy Program said this is a very active time. He said transportation is closely linked to the economic health of cities and affects people’s lives.

In the US, the average commute is getting longer, and roads and bridges are getting old and worn out. Because of this, many US cities must rethink what transportation means. More planners and scientists now see that old plans that focused mostly on cars and fixing traffic jams are not enough. Cities also need to deal with population growth and carbon emissions. Transportation is now a key part of a city’s plans to be more sustainable.

Big US cities like Los Angeles, Seattle, and Chicago are trying to use their streets better. They are adding more bus lanes, improving walking paths, and expanding rail options. At the same time, they are working with private companies on new technology. This technology could let a vehicle drive itself and “talk” to other vehicles and to things around it. This could make transportation more intelligent. Puentes said the most sustainable places to live have multi-modal transport systems, meaning many ways to travel. He also said a city cannot compete globally without a strong transportation network.

Experts say three key trends will shape transportation in the next few years.

Connectivity
Ride-sharing services like Uber and Lyft are like taxis that you book with a smartphone button. Apps like Waze use real-time traffic data to find the fastest routes. These tools are changing how people travel and even how traffic moves through a city. Allan Clelland, an expert in transportation technology, says communication between riders and drivers, between vehicles, and between cars and roads is creating a new age of transportation.

A recent study from UCLA’s Institute of Transportation Studies says that millennials (people born roughly from the early 1980s to the early 2000s) drive less than older generations did. The study says people born in the 1990s make 4% fewer car trips and travel 18% fewer miles per year, on average, than earlier generations did at the same age.

People who still drive may face less traffic because of Waze. Experts say the app has reduced congestion on motorways and shortened travel times. But it has also caused more cars to drive through residential neighbourhoods. Many residents are angry because the extra traffic on quiet streets hurts their quality of life.

This trend may continue as cars share more data with other cars and with roads and traffic systems. Right now, a traffic light can detect that a car is coming, but not much more. Companies are working on technology so a vehicle can tell traffic control systems that it is there, where it is going, and how fast it is moving.

Automation
Driverless cars have been in the news since Google began testing them on roads in 2012. But nobody knows when driverless cars will be common. Still, some automation is already starting. Alexandre Bayen from the University of California expects automation will grow step by step. First, there may be automated buses in special lanes. Later, there may be lorries in ports or mining towns. These would be vehicles that are connected electronically and travel one behind the other.

A fully automated system is interesting because it could make travel safer by reducing human error. It could also make travel more efficient, because car owners could do other tasks during a long commute. It may also reduce carbon emissions and traffic congestion, and give more people access to cars. But even if the technology were ready today, it would still take a long time to become fully automated, because the average age of cars on the road is 11.5 years.

To see what driverless cars might look like, go to: vimeo.com/37751380.

Environmental concern
The world is trying to avoid disastrous climate change. Countries, states, and cities are making strong goals to cut emissions. These policies could change transportation a lot. They could push the use of zero- and low-emission vehicles. They could also support apps that encourage more walking, biking, and carpooling.

Sharon Feigon, executive director of the Shared Use Mobility Center, imagines a future with one transit pass for many kinds of travel in the same day. If a train is delayed, a person could use the same pass to get a city bike, take a shuttle, or rent a vehicle through a car share programme. She said these systems will be put together so it is easy for people to move from one type of transport to another.

When thinking about the future of transportation, it is also important to remember why people travel. They travel to go to work, to see friends or family, or to shop. Technology that reduces the need for trips, like virtual meetings or telecommuting, could also change transportation.

Flying cars once seemed like the top idea for the future. But the new technologies being developed today may be even more advanced. They may also be more useful for dealing with the social and environmental problems we may face in the coming decades.

text:
Scientists studied DNA from a wisdom tooth of a hunter-gatherer from Spain who lived about 7,000 years ago. This gave them a new look at what modern humans were like before farming began.

The man had an unusual mix of traits: blue eyes, black or brown hair, and dark skin. Scientists think he probably could not digest milk well, and he may have had more trouble digesting starchy foods than later farmers.

Farming brought humans and animals closer together, so people likely developed stronger immune systems to fight infections from animals. But the man’s DNA showed he already had some gene changes that help the immune system fight bacteria and viruses. Some of these changes still exist in Europeans today.

Carles Lalueza-Fox led the study at the Institute of Evolutionary Biology in Barcelona. He said many of his early ideas about the results were wrong.

The work began after cave explorers found two skeletons in 2006 in a deep cave system in the Cantabrian Mountains in northwest Spain. The bones were well preserved by the cool cave. Both skeletons were men in their early 30s.

Carbon dating showed the remains were about 7,000 years old, from before farming spread into Europe from the Middle East. Ancient objects at the site also matched this time, including reindeer teeth with holes that were worn on clothing.

Scientists studied the better preserved skeleton. After several failed tries, they rebuilt the man’s entire genome using DNA from the root of a third molar (a wisdom tooth). This was the first complete genome from a modern European who lived before the Neolithic revolution.

The DNA results were surprising. Lalueza-Fox expected light skin, but the man had gene types linked to much darker skin. The man also had blue eyes. This was unexpected because scientists had thought the blue-eye change happened more recently than the changes for lighter skin. The results suggest blue eyes may have appeared first in Europe, while skin was becoming lighter over time during the Mesolithic period.

Because of this, artists may need to change how they draw Mesolithic people. Lalueza-Fox said many drawings show hunter-gatherers with light skin, and almost never show dark skin with blue eyes.

The team compared the ancient genome with the DNA of modern Europeans from different places. They found the closest match was with people in northern Europe, especially Sweden and Finland.

The immune-system gene changes suggest that farming did not cause all the immune gene changes found in Europeans today. Some changes seem to be older than farming. Lalueza-Fox said scientists still do not know what kinds of germs affected these people.

Martin Jones, a professor at Cambridge University, said the immune genes were the most striking result. He said there is a common story that early farming was bad for health because people lived closer to humans and animals, had fewer kinds of food, and had crowded water supplies. He said the study highlights that diseases also mattered before farming, and that is interesting.

text:
Two mothers in South Africa found out they are raising each other’s daughters. The babies were accidentally switched at a hospital in 2010.

Now there is a hard problem. One mother wants to fix the mistake and take back her biological child. The other mother does not want to give up the girl she has raised.

A lawyer named Henk Strydom speaks for one of the mothers. A court order says the mothers cannot be named. He said the mistake is very sad and may not end happily.

Both women gave birth at Tambo Memorial Hospital in Boksburg, east of Johannesburg, on the same day in 2010. At the time, no one noticed anything was wrong.

In 2013, one mother, who is 33 and unemployed, went to court to ask her ex-partner for money to help support her daughter. The man said he was not the father. A DNA test showed he was not the father, and the child was also not the mother’s biological child. The mother was very upset and did not know what to do.

Later, she met the other mother. Since December 2013, they have gone to counselling together, set up by the hospital. They also met their biological daughters.

Strydom said his client saw that the other girl looked like her. He said it was very traumatic for her. She is caring for a child who is not biologically hers, while her own child is with someone else.

The woman became unhappy with the process and went to the children’s court to try to get custody of her biological child. The other mother refused to give up the girl. Strydom agreed to represent the woman for free. The woman also has one older child.

Strydom said it is a tragedy. He said the other mother has raised the girl for four years, so it is understandable she does not want to give her up.

The High Court in Pretoria asked the University of Pretoria’s Centre for Child Law to investigate what is best for the children. In South African law, what is best for the children is the most important rule. The centre must report back within 90 days.

Strydom said it is hard to know what the court will decide. He said fixing this after four years is very difficult. Waiting longer may make it more traumatic. He also said that whatever happens, someone will be unhappy.

He said that he and his client do not want to sue the hospital or the government health department right now. They are helping with the case and providing counselling.

The Centre for Child Law will interview the mothers and fathers, and other people who have an important relationship with the girls. The children and mothers will have full clinical assessments and may see a psychologist.

Karabo Ngidi, a lawyer at the centre, said the decision must be in the children’s best interests. She said biology matters, but it is not the only thing that matters.

She also said the families are Zulu, so Zulu tradition, culture, and customary law will be considered. It is also possible that the ex-partner of the mother taking legal action could be the biological father of the other girl.

This is not the first baby-swap case in South Africa. In 1995, two mothers received money after their sons, born in 1989, were accidentally switched at a Johannesburg hospital.

Other cases happened in other countries. In 2009, in Oregon in the United States, Dee Ann Angell and Kay Rene Reed found out they were mixed up at birth in 1953 when a nurse brought them back after bathing. In 2013, in Japan, a 60-year-old man who was swapped at birth was given compensation. He was raised by a poor family instead of his rich biological parents. He grew up on welfare and became a truck driver. His biological siblings, and the boy raised in his place, went to private secondary schools and universities.

Bruce Laing, a clinical psychologist in Johannesburg, said the long-term effects of a baby swap can be very strong and scary. He said parents might start to feel resentment toward a child who is not theirs. He also said parents might keep thinking, “What if?”

text:
From glow-in-the-dark trees to bike storage under the ground and solar-powered bins, people are trying unusual ideas to make cities better places to live.

Living in a city can be good, but it is often hard to live in a sustainable way. City people deal with pollution, traffic, and less green space. So inventors are trying new ideas. Here are some of the wackier solutions.

Pop-up parks
Some cities look like they are built more for cars than for people. A pop-up park is a simple idea. You take an empty car park, some coins, and one or two plants, and you make a small park for a short time. The PARK(ing) project started as an art experiment in San Francisco and then spread around the world. People have also made temporary city farms and ecology demos. The movement has a day of action every September.

Subterranean storage
Not everyone in cities uses a car. Bikes are becoming more popular. But where can you keep a bike safe? A Tokyo-based engineering firm called Giken made an idea called an “eco-cycle, anti-seismic underground bicycle park.” It is a round storage space that is only seven metres wide, and it goes down into the ground. It can store 204 bikes. Owners can get their bike back by pressing a button. The automatic system brings it up in about 13 seconds.

Glow-in-the-dark trees
When many people think of trees that glow, they think of Christmas lights. But Dutch designer-artist Daan Roosegaarde made a “bioluminescent” plant. This experimental technology mixes DNA from glowing marine bacteria with the chloroplast genome of a plant. This makes a glow like a jellyfish. Trials are happening to make a large-scale version. Roosegaarde hopes it could one day replace normal street lights.

Footfall harvesting
In West Ham in east London, many commuters and shoppers walk across an elevated walkway near the underground station. Most people do not notice that the ground feels a little springy. The rubber surface bends about five millimetres. This helps power the streetlights above. The floor has smart tiles that capture energy from footsteps and turn it into electricity. The UK firm Pavegen made this idea. Pavegen has also installed a similar system at London’s Heathrow Airport and other places.

Supertrees
Singapore’s Gardens by the Bay has man-made trees called “supertrees.” They are steel-framed and can be up to 50 metres high. Flowers and ferns grow on them. Their metal tops can absorb and spread heat. They can collect rainwater and help provide air ventilation for two “climate-controlled biomes” below (large conservatories). Eleven of the 18 trees also have solar panels on their “branches.”

text:
Agios Efstratios is a very remote Greek island. Banks and the government do not pay much attention to it. The mobile phone network cannot use data, and there is no ATM or credit-card machine on the island.

Before Greece had big money problems, people on this quiet island did fine. They did banking at the post office. Every summer, the few dozen rooms to rent were full. Visitors came because they heard from other people about the empty beaches, clear sea, and fresh seafood.

But the island still uses cash, so the closing of banks across Greece hurt it a lot. People had to travel nine hours there and back to the nearest big island to get cash. Greek visitors say they cannot get enough money to come. Mayor Maria Kakali said tourist numbers are down 80% this year. The main village has about 200 people. She said even people who were born on the island and now live in Athens are not coming, even if they have their own homes on the island.

Kakali kept pushing the government and a major Greek bank, and they promised an ATM within weeks. But she thinks it may be too late for this tourist season. Tourism is much bigger than the island’s other two ways to earn money: fishing and agriculture. She said there are almost no reservations in August, when normally people call and ask for a room and there are none left.

This winter might be a little better because 50 workers will stay in the village to expand the harbour. But a bigger problem may come soon. The government said it will end an old tax break for islands.

This lower sales tax was made long ago to help islands. It helped because everything had to be brought in, and it helped tourism be cheaper. Popular islands like Mykonos worry they will not be able to compete with Turkey without the tax break. For Agios Efstratios, it is an even bigger danger. Kakali said if they have to pay a tax of 23%, everyone will die on the island.

Food and fuel already cost more than on the mainland. The island is small, with only three shops, two restaurants, and no official hotel, even in summer. Provatas Costas, a 58-year-old fisherman, said it is an expensive island. He said even milk or bread is bought and sold three or four times before it reaches them, and each middleman takes a profit.

For Agios Efstratios and the nearby larger island of Lemnos, the timing is especially cruel. For years they were less well known partly because the ferries were slow and unreliable. In 2015, the government finally gave the ferry contract to a new, efficient company. More visitors started coming to see the islands, but then the bank controls began.

Atzamis Konstantinos, a travel agent in Lemnos, said it started as the best season in 30 years and, in one week, became the worst. He used to earn €15,000 a month as a captain of petroleum tankers and other large ships, but he came home because he missed Lemnos. He said he always loved the island. When he docked in Piraeus, the port in Athens, he would take his pay and then take the first boat to Lemnos. People called him crazy, but he only wanted to come there.

Lemnos has many wild beaches where you can be almost alone, even in the busiest part of summer. It also has a small nightlife and many cultural sites. It is the eighth largest island in Greece, so it may get the first tax increase in autumn 2015, but it is not as rich as many smaller islands. It has a little over 3,000 beds for visitors, compared with tens of thousands on Rhodes. Its councillors say they will fight the tax rise, but they do not know how. Lemnos Mayor Dimitris Marinakis said that when there is not enough money, people buy less, and then the whole economy gets worse.

The crisis has been very hard on people under 40. Many do two or three jobs, but still cannot afford a family. Young people fear a new wave of people leaving, like the one that sent their grandparents’ friends to America, Australia, and Canada. Katerina Fikari said she is very lucky to work for the local government in Lemnos. She asked what the future is for their generation. She said if you only work to pay bills, it is hard to have dreams.

Mayor Kakali warned that if taxes go up, even more young people will leave. She has used her year in office to improve education on the island, to help families want to stay. Because Agios Efstratios is one of the smallest islands, it has until 2017 before the tax rise is planned, so she hopes Greek politics might still stop it. If not, she plans to go to Athens to remind the government what the tax rise would cost.

She said the government does not pay much attention to the islands of the north Aegean. She said she would take all the school children to the gates of parliament to tell them: “There is still life in these islands”.

text:
Harvey Goldsmith, a top British rock promoter and manager, says the time of very big summer music festivals in Britain, including Glastonbury, may be ending.

Goldsmith has worked with many famous stars, such as the Who, the Rolling Stones, Queen, Madonna, Bob Dylan, and Luciano Pavarotti. He says the biggest problem is that there are not enough new, major bands to replace the older ones.

He spoke at the Hay Festival of Literature and Arts in Powys, Wales. He said the festival circuit has already reached its highest point, about two years ago. He said there are too many festivals, and not enough big artists to be the main act. He said the music business is not creating a new generation of headline bands like the Rolling Stones, Muse, or the Arctic Monkeys.

He said there were about 900 music festival events in the UK between May and September 2014, and they cannot all keep going. He thinks festivals will change into events that include more than music, such as poetry, books, or magic shows. He thinks there will be many smaller festivals that offer something extra, instead of huge fields full of people.

Goldsmith also said the way people listen to music has changed. He said many people do not want to listen to a full album now. He said rock bands used to build their name over several albums. But he said young people often choose one track, then quickly move on to the next. He said pop music is everywhere, and he thinks rock may return, but it will take time.

Goldsmith, 69, said he is working with Robin de Levita, a Dutch producer of the Who’s 1970s rock musical Tommy. They are involved with a new 1,100-seat theatre in Wembley. It is planned to be finished in time for the first stage adaptation of the teen book and movie series The Hunger Games in June 2016.

Goldsmith said de Levita will bring his SceneAround idea to London. In this kind of theatre, the audience seats are on a turntable that slowly rotates. The scenes are built in a circle around the audience, with projections on screens. This idea was first used in an aircraft hangar outside Amsterdam and was very successful.

Goldsmith said he is also planning to bring a production of the Anne Frank story to the new venue. He said it is a completely different way to produce shows.

At Hay, Goldsmith also shared stories from his career. He talked about Keith Moon putting dynamite down a toilet in a Sydney hotel room to try to unblock it. He also described John Lennon having very strong stage fright before a show at Madison Square Garden in 1974. Goldsmith said this fear is common for artists, but when the music starts, they are fine.

Goldsmith also explained why Elvis Presley never performed outside North America. He said Presley’s manager, Colonel Tom Parker, told him over tea that Parker was an illegal Dutch immigrant. Parker did not want to risk leaving the US, so Goldsmith’s efforts to bring Elvis to London failed.

Goldsmith said Freddie Mercury was the most powerful live stage performer he has seen. He said that at Live Aid, Mercury went on stage, saw the audience, and took control of it.

But Goldsmith said a new Queen is still not being formed. He said the industry is not creating the next generation of big, long-lasting artists. He said Coldplay is probably the last band to reach that level, and that was ten years ago.

Goldsmith said that without big headline acts, there cannot be big festival shows. He said even Glastonbury cannot find enough big acts anymore, and he called Glastonbury the top of the festival world. He said the big festival era is really over.

text:
What is it like to see the very last of something? Sudan is the last male northern white rhino on Earth. He lives at Ol Pejeta Conservancy in Kenya with two female northern white rhinos. If Sudan does not have a baby with one of them soon, then no more northern white rhinos will ever be born. Sudan is 42 years old, and attempts to help him breed have not worked. Besides these three, there are only two other northern white rhinos in the world. They are both females and live in zoos.

Sudan is guarded by armed men who stay close to him. This may look like kindness, but it is needed because of violence. Poachers still threaten him. Poachers kill rhinos and cut off their horns to sell them in the Asian medicine market, even though Sudan’s horn has been cut off to try to stop them.

Sudan does not know how rare he is. He walks around the reserve with his guards. His big head shows great strength, but he is still in danger. He seems like an old fighter who is tired and worn down.

Sudan’s body is huge, and his legs look short and strong under him. People have been amazed by rhinos for a very long time. Sudan looks a lot like a rhinoceros that Albrecht Dürer drew in 1515. In 1515, a live Indian rhinoceros was sent by the ruler of Gujarat in India to the king of Portugal. The king then sent it to the Pope, but it died in a shipwreck on the way.

Humans often kill the animals they admire. During the Ice Age, people painted pictures of woolly rhinos in caves in France up to 30,000 years ago. These animals were strong and powerful, but they also seemed gentle. A woolly rhino in Chauvet Cave looks young and full of life. But the same people who painted these rhinos also helped to wipe them out. When the climate changed at the end of the last Ice Age, human spears probably helped finish them.

Today many people care about rhinos, but rhinos are still being killed in large numbers. The northern white rhino is the rarest African rhino. There are many more southern white rhinos and black rhinos. But in some countries, people want rhino horn as a traditional medicine. They believe it can cure many illnesses, from flu to cancer. This belief is causing more poaching. In South Africa, only 13 rhinos were killed by poachers in 2007. In 2014, 1,215 rhinos were killed for their horns. 2015 looks likely to be even worse.

The northern white rhino has been hunted almost to extinction, even with guards and guns. Other African rhinos are also under heavy attack from poachers. The Javan rhinoceros is close to extinction too. India has protected the Indian rhinoceros after it was almost wiped out by British hunters in colonial times, but poaching is still a danger. Rhinos are majestic animals, but humans keep destroying them. Have we learned nothing since the Ice Age?

text:
Cathal Redmond was swimming near the coast of Greece. He took photos with his first underwater camera. He thought they would be great. But when he looked at them on land, the pictures were brown and unclear. He took the photos while holding his breath. He thought the problem was that he did not have enough time to set up the shots. He wanted a little more time underwater to film fish in their natural home.

He decided to make that extra time possible. He invented the Express Dive. It is a refillable device that stores air and is held in the mouth. It lets a person swim underwater for two minutes. It is meant to sit between snorkelling and scuba diving. Snorkelling is simple but you cannot go deep or stay long. Scuba diving lets you breathe underwater, but the equipment is heavy and expensive. The prototype looks like a mix of a scuba mouthpiece and a water bottle. It is still in early testing, with separate parts being tested.

Redmond is 27. He says he wanted people to do more than hold their breath for about 30 seconds. In 2006, he took a scuba-diving course. He loved breathing underwater and watching fish. But he did not enjoy the fins, weight belt, wetsuit, air tank, mask, and other gear.

He says he felt like he had about 50kg strapped to him. Getting into the water felt strange, because he was used to staying on the surface. He says scuba diving can be limiting. Even though you can stay underwater longer, you must plan your whole day around it. You may have 20kg to 50kg of gear, so you cannot just walk on the beach and decide to go in. It takes planning and relearning.

Redmond made the Express Dive during his final-year project for his product design degree at the University of Limerick. The device has two main parts. Above the surface, a fan pulls in air through a vent in the mouthpiece. The air goes around a motor-driven turbine. Then it is pushed through valves and stored in a tank. A light flashes green when the tank is full. When air stops going in, the vent closes. When the user dives, air goes back to the mouth through the mouthpiece.

The light is in front of the user’s eyes. It works like a health bar. It changes from green to red when the air is low. The device holds enough air for two minutes underwater. It takes about the same time to refill. Redmond says the hard part was making it small enough to hold with the teeth, with the compressor, batteries, electronics, and parts for delivering air.

The electrical parts are protected from water inside the casing. The device is recharged using inductive power transfer. This uses an electromagnetic field, like pads that can charge mobile phones without wires, so there are no exposed wires.

Redmond says the mouthpiece feels like a snorkel. He says it is like an extra set of lungs. The user takes a deep breath, then uses that stored air underwater. The prototype is made from high-density foam, aluminium, and silicone. Parts of it have been tested. Redmond says the motor can compress two minutes of air, and the device can be held in the mouth. He has not yet tested it on a diver who is fully underwater for two minutes. But he believes that with enough testing, he can make a working device that will not put swimmers in danger.

Redmond recently came runner-up for the International James Dyson Award. This will give him £5,000 to develop the project further. He says early signs suggest the device might cost £280. It may weigh from 1kg to 3kg, depending on the safety features needed.

Some people may think two minutes is not much more than snorkelling. Redmond says it could matter a lot underwater. He says a typical swimmer can hold their breath for about 40 seconds underwater. He says two minutes is not a lot, but it is much longer than 40 seconds.

text:
It is not a secret that many millennials use a lot of technology. More than 8 out of 10 say they sleep with a mobile phone next to their bed. Almost 2 out of 3 say they text while driving. About 1 out of 5 has posted a video of themselves online. And 3 out of 4 have made a profile on a social networking site.

A 2010 report says millennials use social media more than other generations. About 75% of millennials have at least one social media account. Only 50% of Generation X, 30% of baby boomers, and 6% of people age 65 and older use social media. But some millennials do not use social media at all. Here are a few of them.

Celan Beausoleil, 31, Oakland, California
Beausoleil is a social worker. She has used Facebook on and off, but mostly off. She last turned off her account in December 2015. She did this because she felt it was too hard to see so much personal information from other people, on top of her work.

She said her job is listening to people’s lives all day. Then going online and seeing every detail of many people’s lives felt overwhelming, even for people she did not know well. She said it felt like “too much closeness.” She also said she already carries a lot from her work, and it felt too heavy to carry that online too.

But she liked how social media can connect people around the world. She said one reason she stayed on Facebook was that she could click from one friend to another and end up on someone’s page on the other side of the world. She said she used to do that a lot.

Jason Mathias, 26, Baltimore, Maryland
Mathias said he one day noticed he was spending a lot of time on social media. He thought that small seconds add up. He wondered what life would be like if he used those seconds for other things.

Mathias works for the Baltimore City government. He had Facebook and Twitter for years, but he deleted both in November 2012. After that, he quickly forgot about Facebook.

He still understands some good things about social media. For example, when meeting his girlfriend’s friends, it can help to remember people by looking at photos and learning where they are from and what they do. It is also easy to plan big events online. Mathias now depends on friends to invite him to parties, and he thinks sometimes he misses things.

But he enjoys having more free time. In elevator rides and short breaks at work, he reads news and books instead of scrolling online. And because he cannot follow friends online, he has to call them on the phone, and he says he really enjoys that.

Lauren Raskauskas, 22, Naples, Florida
Raskauskas says she is a private person. Social media does not feel good to her because other people can watch and judge what you share. She worries about giving out her data. She is currently looking for a job.

She deleted her Twitter account and turned off her Facebook account two years ago. She did this after she realized she did not like everyone knowing what she was doing. She joined Facebook later than many people because her parents were very strict about technology.

She can still see good parts of social media. One time, a friend she had lost touch with moved to Naples for a month. Raskauskas did not know her friend was there until after she left, and she said that was a bummer.

In the end, Raskauskas cared more about privacy than the benefits of social media. She also saw a good side after a break-up. The last time she broke up with someone while using Facebook, she worried about things like changing her profile photo and status. This time, she did not have to think about any of that.

Hariharan Rajagopalan, 18, Boston, Massachusetts
Rajagopalan is a student at Boston College. He does not think there are bad results from not using social media. He says he has not seen any effect so far. Even when classmates post about parties and events on Facebook, they also send him a text message.

He said that since he was young, he was usually a step behind on those things, so it did not matter to him.

The only time he used social media was when he had to. Before his first year of college, he joined Facebook so he could contact his new roommate and talk about plans for the year.

Months later, he still has the account, but he says he does not use it or check it. The only activity is when his two sisters tag him in family photos. He has not made other social media accounts. He says Twitter is tempting because sports news often breaks there, but he did not make an account because he does not need one to read tweets.

text:
Most of B.B. King’s work was blues, and much of his best work was blues. But he was always willing to play in other places too. He was friendly and skilled, and he helped connect different kinds of music and different cultures. It may be too soon to call him “the last of the bluesmen,” but it is hard to imagine another blues artist having as much influence as King. He influenced thousands of musicians and millions of listeners during a 65-year career.

Riley B. King was born near Itta Bena, Mississippi. The “B” did not seem to stand for a name. He grew up with few chances in life because he was an African-American farm worker. Little by little, he worked past this barrier. A family friend taught him the basics of guitar. He also improved his singing by singing with a quartet of gospel singers. In his early 20s, he moved to Memphis.

After a couple of years, he played often at a bar in West Memphis, Arkansas. He also became a disc jockey on a Memphis radio station. He was called “The Beale Street Blues Boy.” This was shortened to “Blues Boy King,” and then to “BB.” After one recording session in 1949 for a Nashville label, King began recording for Modern Records in 1950.

His first hit was in 1952. It was a new, dramatic version of “Three O’Clock Blues.” It was number one on the R&B chart for 15 weeks. More successful songs followed, such as “Please Love Me,” “You Upset Me Baby,” and “Sweet Sixteen.” On these and many other recordings, most of them his own songs, King built a style that was new but still clearly part of blues history. He often praised the musicians who influenced him and usually named T-Bone Walker first.

King told Guitar Player magazine, “I’ve tried my best to get that sound. I came pretty close but never quite got it.” In an interview in 2001, he said, “If T-Bone Walker had been a woman I would have asked him to marry me.” He also named older blues guitarists Blind Lemon Jefferson and Lonnie Johnson, and jazz players Charlie Christian and Django Reinhardt.

King said part of his guitar style came from what he could not do well. He said he started bending notes because he could not play in the bottleneck style, like Elmore James and Booker White. He loved that sound but could not do it. He also spoke modestly about his singing. His singing mixed smooth singers like Nat King Cole with loud blues singers like Joe Turner and Dr Clayton. He especially liked the composer and singer Louis Jordan, and he honored him in the 1999 album Let the Good Times Roll.

During the 1950s, King was the top blues artist playing in black-supported theatres and clubs. He played many one-night shows. In 1956, he was said to have played 342 engagements. In 1962, he tried to change his work life by signing with a major label, ABC. But the first records under that contract tried to make him a mainstream pop singer. His fans did not like them, and ABC’s accountants did not either.

His 1965 album Live at the Regal showed how strong his blues music was. It became a famous album and an important early influence for many younger musicians. He had more R&B hits with blues songs like “How Blue Can You Get” and “Paying the Cost to Be the Boss.” In 1969, he reached high on the pop charts with “The Thrill Is Gone,” something blues artists had not done for many years.

It took time for him to become popular with rock audiences. Many rock fans thought of blues mainly through Chicago blues artists like Muddy Waters and Howlin’ Wolf. But rock musicians who admired King helped more people notice him. In 1969 he said that kids started calling him the greatest blues guitarist in the world, and when he asked who told them that, they said “Mike Bloomfield” or “Eric Clapton.” He said he owed his new popularity to these young people. He gained more rock respect with the 1970 album Indianola Mississippi Seeds, where he worked with Carole King and Joe Walsh.

After that, King was firmly known as “the chairman of the board of blues singers.” With help from his manager, Sidney Seidenberg, he toured internationally, including Japan, Australia, China, and Russia. He also played concerts for prisoners at the Cook County jail in Chicago and at San Quentin. These experiences led to his long involvement in rehabilitation programmes.

In 1990, King was diagnosed with diabetes. He toured less, but he still toured enough that fans outside the US could see him every year or two. He often performed seated, but his singing and guitar playing stayed strong and only slowly weakened. For his 80th birthday in 2005, there were big celebrations. They included an award-winning album with collaborations with Eric Clapton, Mark Knopfler, Roger Daltrey, Gloria Estefan, and others. Many musicians, including Bono, Amadou Bagayoko, and Elton John, also paid tribute. There was also a “farewell tour,” but it did not end up being a real farewell.

In 2009, King won a Grammy award for best traditional blues album for One Kind Favor. In 2012, he performed at the White House. The US President, Barack Obama, joined him to sing “Sweet Home Chicago.” King was married twice and divorced twice. He had 11 children with different partners. Four other children died before he did.

text:
Seeing co-workers and other people use an e-cigarette is now common. But have we reached “peak vape”? Some statistics suggest vaping may already be going down among smokers and recent ex-smokers. These people are most vapers. Big e-cigarette companies will watch these numbers closely, because they have spent millions promoting a product that was expected to keep growing.

In 2014, the health charity Action on Smoking and Health (ASH) released figures about adults in Britain. It said e-cigarette use tripled from about 700,000 users in 2012 to 2.1 million in 2014. E-cigarettes do not contain tobacco. They make vapour, not smoke.

But other figures from the Smoking Toolkit Study suggest interest may be getting weaker. This research group is backed by the Department of Health and reports on smoking trends every three months. It found that vaping among smokers and ex-smokers rose steadily until the end of 2013. At that time, about 22% of smokers and ex-smokers were vaping. During 2014, this stayed about the same, and then fell to 19% in the last three months of 2014. Early signs suggest the fall continued into 2015. Professor Robert West, from UCL’s Department of Epidemiology and Public Health, said this drop is “statistically significant.” He collects the Toolkit figures.

Smokers are the main target for e-cigarette companies because 7 out of 10 vapers are smokers. Only about 1% of people who have never smoked have tried an e-cigarette. West said the number of people who use e-cigarettes while still smoking is going down. He also said the team has only tracked vaping for just over a year, so this is a short time. They are not seeing growth in long-term ex-smokers or “never” smokers using e-cigarettes. He said rates could change, but right now they look steady.

This flat growth in the UK seems different from the US, where e-cigarettes have been promoted strongly and reports said they were growing. But West questioned the US data, because it did not clearly separate people who tried an e-cigarette once from people who vape regularly.

Experts say worries that vaping will become trendy among young non-smokers seem wrong. ASH found only 1.8% of children are regular users. Instead, e-cigarettes seem most popular with adults who want to quit smoking. James Dunworth, director and co-founder of ecigarettedirect.co.uk, said that even though Smoking In England shows use by smokers has levelled off, it also shows a big rise since May 2011. He said customers are happy, and that better technology and hardware are improving the experience and helping people switch from traditional cigarettes.

West agreed and said e-cigarettes act like a “souped-up nicotine patch.” He said they are more popular than nicotine patches, and may or may not be more effective. He said one-third of quit attempts use e-cigarettes, making them the most popular way to try to stop.

Hazel Cheeseman, policy director at ASH, said it is too early to say if vaping has peaked. She said the market in the UK may not have grown for about a year, but there does not seem to be a fall in the number of people using e-cigarettes to help them quit. She said using an e-cigarette is safer than smoking. Some, but not all, people find them helpful to quit. She also said there is little evidence they are causing more young people to smoke.

Recently, it was reported that the European Commission (EC) is considering higher taxes on e-cigarettes. This could affect their popularity. A new EC tobacco directive starts in 2016. It will limit nicotine in e-cigarettes to below current levels. This may mean vapers will need to use them more to get the same effect, which could also make e-cigarettes more expensive.

West said policymakers should treat e-cigarettes as a way to help people stop smoking, and not regulate them the same way as smoking. He said some local authorities and organizations treat e-cigarettes like cigarettes and ban them in public places and outdoors. He said this harms public health messages, because it can sound like an attack on vapers and uses an “everyone with a sledgehammer” approach. He said people must be careful not to stigmatize e-cigarettes.

text:
Vienna is the best city in the world to live in. Baghdad is the worst. London, Paris, and New York are not in the top 35. This is from an international study about quality of life.

In the 18th Mercer Quality of Life study, many German-speaking cities are near the top. Vienna is number one. Zurich, Munich, Dusseldorf, and Frankfurt are also in the top seven.

Paris fell ten places to 37th place. London is 39th. Mercer says this is mostly because Paris is more at risk of terrorist attacks.

The study looked at many things, like social and economic conditions, health, education, housing, and the environment. Big companies use this study to help decide where to work and how much to pay workers.

Helena Hartlauer, 32, was born in Vienna. She said she is not surprised Vienna is number one. She said Vienna has a long tradition of building good social housing. This makes Vienna cheaper to live in than many big cities.

Hartlauer said she lives in a 100 square metre old apartment in a good area, about 20 minutes’ walk from the city centre. Her rent is 800 euros (£625) a month. She said a similar apartment in London would cost more than £2,000. It would cost even more in New York, which is 44th.

Cities in the US did not do very well in the study. Mercer says this is mostly because of personal safety and crime. The highest US city is San Francisco at 28th. Boston is 34th. Canadian cities did much better, led by Vancouver.

Hartlauer said Vienna feels very safe. She also said Vienna has very good public transport. The underground runs 24 hours on weekends. One trip costs 1 euro.

Vienna did very well after the Berlin Wall fell. It became an important place for travel and business with Eastern European countries.

Martin Eichtinger, the Austrian ambassador to London, lived in Vienna for 20 years. He said Vienna’s biggest strength is its location. He said the fall of the Berlin Wall helped make Vienna a center for companies that want to do business in Central Europe.

The World Bank says Austria has one of the highest GDP per head in the world. It is just behind the US and ahead of Germany and Britain. It is still below Switzerland.

Mercer says Zurich has the second highest quality of life. But people from Vienna say their city is more fun. Hartlauer said Vienna has more students than any other German-speaking city. She said it is growing fast and is young and lively. She also said she works for Vienna’s tourist board.

Many British tourists have not visited Vienna much. They often go to Barcelona or Berlin, and they often think of Austria as a place for skiing, lakes, and mountains.

But Vienna is becoming more popular with British visitors. There are more budget flights from cities like Manchester and Edinburgh. In 2015, 588,000 British people visited Vienna. This was 18% more than the year before. Eichtinger said London is now the number one city destination for Austrian visitors.

Mercer said Vienna has been number one in the last seven published rankings. Mercer said Vienna scores high because it is safe and stable, has good public services and transport, and has good places for free time.

Eichtinger said the European migrant crisis has had little effect on Vienna, a city of nearly 1.8 million people. He said Austria has taken in 90,000 refugees, but the numbers have slowed in recent months.

Mercer says London has never been in the top ten. London scores poorly for air pollution, traffic congestion, and climate. After London, Edinburgh is the next British city on the list, in 46th place.

Paris had the biggest fall in the newest rankings. Mercer said Paris had stayed stable for several years, but this year it dropped ten places. Mercer said the main reason was the terrorist attacks in 2015. Mercer also said safety is very important in the scoring, so even small changes can change the ranking a lot.

Auckland in New Zealand was the highest English-speaking city, in third place. Vancouver was fifth. Australian cities also did very well: Sydney was 10th and Melbourne was 15th. The Economist has often ranked Melbourne as the most liveable city, but some people say its survey focuses too much on English-speaking places.

War and political unrest are the main reasons for the worst-ranked cities. Damascus was only the seventh worst. It ranked better than Baghdad and also better than Bangui in Central African Republic, Sana’a in Yemen, Port-au-Prince in Haiti, Khartoum in Sudan, and N’Djamena in Chad.
